 216: +
 217: +## §4 — Sovereignty + audit standards
 218: +
 219: +- /transparency lists T1-only cleared tiers per CLAUDE.md §9.1 invariant 1
 220: +- Bundle's verified_report has zero T2+ sources cited in passing sentences
 221: +- Bundle's manifest.yaml decision_id + report_id + pool_id chain matches the source artifact directory
 222: +- egress_lockdown.sh has been run on the EC2 host (operator confirms via SSM session)
 223: +
 224: +VERDICT: PASS / FAIL on each.
 225: +
 226: +## §5 — Halt conditions per CLAUDE.md §3.0
 227: +
 228: +Operator confirms NONE of these fired in the last 24h:
 229: +- canonical pin SHA mismatch
 230: +- CHARTER.md / PLAN.md SHA pin mismatch
 231: +- Issue jump attempt
 232: +- PR opened with missing artifact triple
 233: +- Codex unavailable > 1h
 234: +- 2-cycle repeated root cause
 235: +- 200-LOC PR cap exceeded without exemption
 236: +- 3+ PRs queued for user in 24h (reviewer fatigue)
 237: +
 238: +VERDICT: ALL CLEAR / SOME FIRED.
 239: +
 240: +## §6 — Output schema
 241: +
 242: +```yaml
 243: +verdict: APPROVE | REQUEST_CHANGES
 244: +ship_decision: SHIP | HALT
 245: +sections_passed: [§1, §2, §3a, §3b, §3c, §3d, §4, §5]
 246: +sections_failed: [...]
 247: +blocking_concerns: [...]
 248: +non_blocking_concerns_recorded_as_followup_issues: [...]
 249: +```
 250: diff --git a/docs/carney_demo_runbook.md b/docs/carney_demo_runbook.md
 251: new file mode 100644
 252: index 00000000..f838d766
 253: --- /dev/null
 254: +++ b/docs/carney_demo_runbook.md
 255: @@ -0,0 +1,162 @@
 256: +# Carney demo runbook — operator playbook
 257: +
 258: +This is the single source of truth for the PM Mark Carney POLARIS demo. Read end-to-end before the meeting; refer back during.
 259: +
 260: +**Demo target window:** 2026-06-05 to 2026-06-09. Updated 2026-05-13 after all I-arch-001* + I-carney-002..005 PRs merged.
 261: +
 262: +---
 263: +
 264: +## §0 — Prereqs (1 week before demo)
 265: +
 266: +| Item | Owner | Status |
 267: +|---|---|---|
 268: +| AWS account with ca-central-1 IAM admin | Ops | (verify) |
 269: +| Route 53 public hosted zone | Ops | (verify) |
 270: +| `terraform` + `gh` + `aws` CLIs installed | Ops | `terraform --version && gh --version && aws --version` |
 271: +| Demo signing GPG key generated | Ops | `bash scripts/bootstrap_gpg_demo_key.sh` |
 272: +| OpenRouter + Serper API keys procured | Ops | private records |
 273: +| Carney office contact + demo time confirmed | Lead | calendar invite |
 274: +| Fallback laptop ready | Lead | `docker compose -f docker-compose.v6.yml up -d` on laptop |
 275: +
 276: +## §1 — Deploy day-1 (T-7 before demo)
 277: +
 278: +```bash
 279: +cd infra/aws
 280: +cp terraform.tfvars.example terraform.tfvars
 281: +# Edit terraform.tfvars: domain_name, route53_zone_name, polaris_repo_commit
 282: +# (current polaris HEAD SHA), openrouter/serper keys, polaris_gpg_key_id,
 283: +# polaris_gpg_pubkey, static_accounts_yaml (bcrypt hashes), gpg_private_key_armored.
 284: +
 285: +aws s3 mb s3://polaris-carney-tf-state --region ca-central-1
 286: +aws s3api put-bucket-versioning \
 287: +    --bucket polaris-carney-tf-state \
 288: +    --versioning-configuration Status=Enabled
 289: +
 290: +terraform init \
 291: +    -backend-config="bucket=polaris-carney-tf-state" \
 292: +    -backend-config="region=ca-central-1" \
 293: +    -backend-config="key=polaris-carney/terraform.tfstate"
 294: +
 295: +terraform plan -out plan.bin
 296: +terraform apply plan.bin
 297: +```
 298: +
 299: +Wait ~10 minutes for EC2 + ACM + cloud-init. Verify:
 300: +
 301: +```bash
 302: +curl -fsS https://polaris.<your-domain>/health
 303: +curl -fsS https://polaris.<your-domain>/transparency | jq
 304: +```
 305: +
 306: +## §2 — Smoke test (T-3 before demo)
 307: +
 308: +Authenticate, submit a clinical question, verify the bundle round-trips:
 309: +
 310: +```bash
 311: +TOKEN=$(curl -fsS -X POST https://polaris.<your-domain>/api/v6/auth/login \
 312: +    -H 'content-type: application/json' \
 313: +    -d '{"username":"carney_office","password":"<reviewer-pw>"}' \
 314: +    | jq -r .access_token)
 315: +
 316: +# Submit a run.
 317: +RUN_ID=$(curl -fsS -X POST https://polaris.<your-domain>/api/v6/runs \
 318: +    -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
 319: +    -H 'content-type: application/json' \
 320: +    -d '{"template":"clinical","question":"Is tirzepatide effective for type 2 diabetes?"}' \
 321: +    | jq -r .run_id)
 322: +echo "Run: $RUN_ID"
 323: +
 324: +# Tail the event stream.
 325: +curl -N -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
 326: +    https://polaris.<your-domain>/api/v6/stream/$RUN_ID
 327: +
 328: +# Fetch the signed bundle.
 329: +curl -fsS -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
 330: +    -o bundle.tar.gz \
 331: +    https://polaris.<your-domain>/api/v6/runs/$RUN_ID/bundle.tar.gz
 332: +tar -xzf bundle.tar.gz
 333: +
 334: +# Verify the GPG signature from the operator workstation (NOT from inside
 335: +# the deploy — the whole point is independent verification).
 336: +cd audit_*/
 337: +curl -fsS https://polaris.<your-domain>/transparency/pubkey.asc | gpg --import
 338: +gpg --verify manifest.yaml.asc manifest.yaml
 339: +```
 340: +
 341: +Expect: `Good signature from "POLARIS Carney Demo <signing@polaris.local>"`.
 342: +
 343: +## §3 — Live-submission rehearsal (T-2 before demo, I-carney-006)
 344: +
 345: +Per `state/polaris_restart/issue_breakdown.md` I-carney-006 acceptance: run 5 canonical clinical/policy questions PLUS 5 staff-style questions that Carney's office is likely to ask. For EACH submitted run, complete the line-by-line §-1.1 audit per CLAUDE.md before the demo:
 346: +
 347: +| # | Question | Domain | Expected gate |
 348: +|---|---|---|---|
 349: +| 1 | Is tirzepatide effective for type 2 diabetes? | clinical | success or partial_qwen_advisory |
 350: +| 2 | What is Canada's federal pharmacare proposal status? | policy | success |
 351: +| 3 | How does the NORAD modernization plan affect Arctic sovereignty? | defense → policy fallback | success |
 352: +| 4 | What does Canada's AI sovereignty strategy say about model residency? | ai_sovereignty | success |
 353: +| 5 | What are the workforce implications of the Canada-US digital services tax? | workforce | success |
 354: +| 6-10 | Staff-style softer-phrased variations of the above | mixed | mix of success + abort_corpus_inadequate |
 355: +
 356: +For each: audit the bundle per CLAUDE.md §-1.1 (line-by-line claim against cited span). VERIFIED / PARTIAL / UNSUPPORTED / FABRICATED / UNREACHABLE per claim. Compile into `outputs/audits/carney_demo_rehearsal_<date>/` for the demo binder.
 357: +
 358: +## §4 — Live demo (T=0)
 359: +
 360: +1. Open https://polaris.<your-domain>/ in browser, log in as `carney_office`.
 361: +2. Show the home / intake screen.
 362: +3. Submit Q1 (tirzepatide) — narrate the scope gate + retrieval + verification phases as SSE events stream.
 363: +4. When the run completes, click into the Inspector — show source-tier mix, verified-sentence Inspector pane.
 364: +5. Download the bundle — verify the signature on operator laptop (NOT in browser; demo independence).
 365: +6. Navigate to `/transparency` — read aloud the sovereignty filter, evaluator models, egress allowlist. Hand Carney's office the URL.
 366: +7. Submit Q2 (pharmacare) — observe sovereignty cascade in real time.
 367: +8. Take questions.
 368: +
 369: +## §5 — Fallback laptop
 370: +
 371: +If the AWS deploy is unreachable (DNS, ALB down, etc.) during the demo:
 372: +
 373: +```bash
 374: +cd /local/polaris
 375: +docker compose -f docker-compose.v6.yml down
 376: +docker compose -f docker-compose.v6.yml up -d
 377: +```
 378: +
 379: +Localhost demo at http://localhost:3000. **This requires you to have set POLARIS_JWT_SECRET locally** — pull it from Secrets Manager via the operator's AWS CLI BEFORE the demo, write it to `/etc/polaris/.env` on the laptop.
 380: +
 381: +## §6 — 30-minute internal rehearsal (T-1 before demo)
 382: +
 383: +Schedule a 30-min Zoom with at least one non-engineer:
 384: +- They follow the demo script as the "Carney's office" surrogate.
 385: +- Time each step. Target: full demo < 20 min (10 min buffer for questions).
 386: +- Verify the bundle GPG verify on their workstation (not yours).
 387: +- Verify the /transparency page is readable.
 388: +- Pad any unclear narration.
 389: +
 390: +## §7 — Codex sign-off (T-1 before demo)
 391: +
 392: +Run `env -u OPENAI_API_KEY codex exec --skip-git-repo-check - < .codex/I-carney-007/carney_demo_signoff_brief.md`. The brief instructs Codex to:
 393: +1. Verify all I-arch-001a..001f PRs merged (per `state/active_issue.json`)
 394: +2. Verify all I-carney-002, 003, 004, 005, 007 PRs merged
 395: +3. Confirm `/transparency` and `/health` are reachable (operator runs the curls and pastes outputs)
 396: +4. Confirm the bundle signature verifies on at least 2 questions
 397: +
 398: +Expected verdict: `verdict: APPROVE convergence_call: accept_remaining`. Anything REQUEST_CHANGES → escalate to I-carney-006 (live rehearsal) for the failing question.
 399: +
 400: +## §8 — Post-demo tear-down
 401: +
 402: +```bash
 403: +# Recover audit bundles BEFORE destroy.
 404: +aws s3 sync s3://polaris-carney-audit-<acct-id>/ ./carney_demo_bundles/
 405: +
 406: +# Tear down the AWS stack.
 407: +cd infra/aws
 408: +terraform destroy
 409: +```
 410: +
 411: +## §9 — Known limitations (disclose during demo if asked)
 412: +
 413: +- Single-AZ EC2 (no HA pair) — for demo window only
 414: +- Manual GPG private key rotation (Secrets Manager rotation_lambda is Phase-2)
 415: +- Pipeline-A only (pipeline-B legacy + pipeline-C frozen per architecture.md §5)
 416: +- 18-domain egress allowlist enforced via iptables (not VPC NACLs — also defense-in-depth Phase-2)
 417: +- /docs + /redoc + /openapi.json public for operator ergonomics — gate to admin in Phase-2

1760: # POLARIS v6 deploy runbook (Carney demo)
1761: 
1762: **Scope:** single-host `docker compose` deployment for the Carney demo. Production AWS Canada Central infrastructure is tracked separately in **I-carney-002**.
1763: 
1764: ## Prereqs
1765: 
1766: - Docker Engine + Compose v2 (`docker compose version` returns 2.x)
1767: - GPG installed on host (`gpg --version` returns 2.2+)
1768: - A `.env` file at repo root with at minimum:
1769: 
1770: ```
1771: OPENROUTER_API_KEY=<your-key>
1772: SERPER_API_KEY=<your-key>
1773: SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY=<optional>
1774: POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID=<set after Step 1>
1775: POLARIS_GPG_HOMEDIR=~/.gnupg-polaris
1776: POLARIS_API_PORT=8000
1777: POLARIS_WEB_PORT=3000
1778: PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN=5.00
1779: ```
1780: 
1781: `.env` is gitignored; do NOT commit it. A skeleton lives at `.env.example` (added in I-carney-004).
1782: 
1783: ## Step 1 — Bootstrap the GPG demo signing key
1784: 
1785: ```
1786: bash scripts/bootstrap_gpg_demo_key.sh
1787: ```
1788: 
1789: Idempotent: skips if the `POLARIS Carney Demo` key already exists under `$GNUPGHOME`. On success it prints the fingerprint and writes the public key to `outputs/polaris_demo_pubkey.asc`. Copy the printed `POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID=<fingerprint>` line into your `.env`.
1790: 
1791: ## Step 2 — Bring up the stack
1792: 
1793: ```
1794: docker compose -f docker-compose.v6.yml up -d --build
1795: ```
1796: 
1797: This builds the v6 backend image (`Dockerfile.v6`) and the Next.js webui image (`web/Dockerfile`), then starts four services: `redis`, `api`, `worker`, `webui`.
1798: 
1799: Watch the startup:
1800: 
1801: ```
1802: docker compose -f docker-compose.v6.yml logs -f
1803: ```
1804: 
1805: `api` and `worker` will wait for `redis` healthcheck before they start. Expect `[entrypoint] redis reachable` then `[entrypoint] starting uvicorn` (api) or `starting dramatiq worker` (worker).
1806: 
1807: ## Step 3 — Smoke test
1808: 
1809: ```
1810: # Backend health
1811: curl -fsS http://localhost:8000/health
1812: 
1813: # Webui
1814: curl -fsS http://localhost:3000/
1815: 
1816: # I-carney-004: auth-gated endpoints require a Bearer JWT. Get one first:
1817: TOKEN=$(curl -fsS -X POST http://localhost:8000/auth/login \
1818:     -H 'content-type: application/json' \
1819:     -d '{"username":"carney_office","password":"<your-password>"}' \
1820:     | jq -r .access_token)
1821: 
1822: # Submit a sample run (browser-side fetch lands here via /api/v6/runs)
1823: curl -fsS -X POST http://localhost:8000/runs \
1824:     -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
1825:     -H 'content-type: application/json' \
1826:     -d '{"template":"clinical","question":"Is tirzepatide effective for type 2 diabetes?"}'
1827: ```
1828: 
1829: The response is a `RunStatusResponse` with `run_id` (UUID), `lifecycle_status="queued"`. Within a few seconds the Dramatiq worker picks it up and pipeline-A runs.
1830: 
1831: Stream events:
1832: 
1833: ```
1834: curl -N http://localhost:8000/stream/<run_id>
1835: ```
1836: 
1837: ## Step 4 — Rollback
1838: 
1839: ```
1840: docker compose -f docker-compose.v6.yml down
1841: ```
1842: 
1843: For a clean wipe (drops Redis AOF + sqlite v6_runs.sqlite — DATA LOSS):
1844: 
1845: ```
1846: docker compose -f docker-compose.v6.yml down -v
1847: ```
1848: 
1849: ## Troubleshooting
1850: 
1851: | Symptom | Likely cause | Fix |
1852: |---|---|---|
1853: | `api` keeps restarting; logs show `redis unreachable after 10s` | Redis service hasn't initialized | `docker compose logs redis` — usually first-run AOF setup; wait 30s |
1854: | 503 `gpg_unavailable` from `/runs/{id}/bundle.tar.gz` | `POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID` empty or key missing | Re-run Step 1; verify `docker compose exec api gpg --list-keys` shows the key |
1855: | Webui returns 502 from `/api/v6/*` | Internal proxy can't reach `api` service | `docker compose exec webui wget --spider http://api:8000/health` |
1856: | `state/v6_runs.sqlite` not writable | `shared_state` volume permissions | `docker compose exec api ls -la /app/state`; rebuild volume |
1857: | Pipeline-A 400 `cited_span_unreachable_after_snapshot` | Evidence pool full_text shorter than cited span | Check `outputs/<run_id>/evidence_pool.json` snippet length |
1858: 
1859: For env-var diagnostics:
1860: 
1861: ```
1862: docker compose -f docker-compose.v6.yml exec api /entrypoint.sh preflight
1863: ```
1864: 
1865: This runs `scripts/v6_preflight.py` which checks env vars, redis reachability, GPG keyring, and run_store write perms — exits non-zero with a checklist of failures.
1866: 
1867: ## Reference
1868: 
1869: - I-carney-005 Codex artifacts: `.codex/I-carney-005/` (brief iters 1-5, diff iter 1+)
1870: - I-arch-001a..001f chain (run_store → SSE → bundle): merged in PRs #475-#480
1871: 
1872: 
1873:  succeeded in 1056ms:
1874: # POLARIS Carney demo — public transparency reference
1875: 
1876: This document tells reviewers (incl. PM Mark Carney's office) how to audit that this POLARIS deploy is sovereign, signed, and bound by the policies it claims.
1877: 
1878: The machine-readable companion is `GET /transparency` on the live deploy.
1879: 
1880: ## 1. Sovereignty filter
1881: 
1882: POLARIS' clinical-grade filter accepts only **Tier 1 (T1)** sources by default. T1 is the regulatory + clinical guideline corpus (FDA, EMA, NICE, Health Canada, etc.) marked `legal_cleared` in the evidence pool.
1883: 
1884: Sentences citing non-T1 sources are **redacted** from the bundle. If a section has zero passing sentences after the cascade, the section is **dropped**. If a report has zero passing sections, the pipeline raises `SovereigntyFilterEmptiedReportError` (HTTP 422 from `/runs/{id}/bundle.tar.gz`).
1885: 
1886: Tier definitions:
1887: 
1888: | Tier | Description | Default sovereignty status |
1889: |---|---|---|
1890: | T1 | Regulatory + clinical guideline corpora | legal_cleared = true |
1891: | T2 | Peer-reviewed primary literature | requires explicit operator clearance |
1892: | T3 | Unverified web content + raw uploads | excluded; fallback on unknown tiers |
1893: 
1894: Verify: `curl https://polaris.<domain>/transparency/policy` returns the `sovereignty_filter` block.
1895: 
1896: ## 2. Evaluator models
1897: 
1898: Pipeline-A uses a **two-family evaluator**: generator and verifier are from different training lineages so an internal failure in one family can't pass the other.
1899: 
1900: Current models in production (from `/transparency`):
1901: - **Generator:** `${PG_GENERATOR_MODEL}` (default `deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro`)
1902: - **Evaluator:** `${PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL}` (default `qwen/qwen-2.5-72b-instruct`)
1903: 
1904: `openrouter_client.check_family_segregation` raises `RuntimeError` at construction if the configured models are not from different families.
1905: 
1906: ## 3. GPG signing — verifying a bundle
1907: 
1908: Every audit bundle returned by `GET /runs/{run_id}/bundle.tar.gz` contains a `manifest.yaml.asc` GPG detached signature over `manifest.yaml`. Verify on your workstation:
1909: 
1910: ```bash
1911: # Fetch the demo signing public key.
1912: curl -fsS https://polaris.<domain>/transparency/pubkey.asc > polaris.pubkey.asc
1913: gpg --import polaris.pubkey.asc
1914: 
1915: # Fetch a bundle.
1916: curl -fsS https://polaris.<domain>/api/v6/runs/<run_id>/bundle.tar.gz > bundle.tar.gz
1917: tar -xzf bundle.tar.gz
1918: cd audit_*/
1919: 
1920: # Verify the signature over manifest.yaml.
1921: gpg --verify manifest.yaml.asc manifest.yaml
1922: ```
1923: 
1924: A successful verification prints `Good signature from "POLARIS Carney Demo <signing@polaris.local>"`. The fingerprint must match the `signing_key_fingerprint` field in `/transparency`.
1925: 
1926: ## 4. Egress allowlist
1927: 
1928: The EC2 host runs `scripts/egress_lockdown.sh` which installs iptables rules in BOTH `OUTPUT` (host-originated) and `DOCKER-USER` (container-forwarded) chains. Off-allowlist traffic on 80/443 is **dropped + logged via the kernel iptables LOG target** with prefix `[POLARIS-EGRESS-DROP]`. The script's own install/config events go to `/var/log/polaris-egress.log`; the drop log lines land wherever rsyslog/journald is configured to forward kernel facility messages (typically `/var/log/kern.log` or `journalctl -k`).
1929: 
1930: Current allowlist domains (from `/transparency`):
1931: 
1932: - `openrouter.ai`, `api.openrouter.ai` — LLM API
1933: - `google.serper.dev` — live retrieval (Serper)
1934: - `api.semanticscholar.org` — Semantic Scholar
1935: - `github.com`, `codeload.github.com` — source clones
1936: - `registry-1.docker.io`, `auth.docker.io`, `production.cloudflare.docker.com` — Docker registry
1937: - `ssm.ca-central-1.amazonaws.com` + `ssmmessages.ca-central-1.amazonaws.com` + `ec2messages.ca-central-1.amazonaws.com` — SSM Session Manager
1938: - `s3.ca-central-1.amazonaws.com` — audit bundle upload
1939: 
1940: Build-time hosts (`pypi.org`, `files.pythonhosted.org`, `deb.debian.org`, `security.debian.org`, `registry.npmjs.org`) are in the allowlist for first-boot image build; operators tighten further by removing them post-build. (Full count: 18 entries in `config/egress_allowlist.txt` including `security.debian.org`.)
1941: 
1942: DNS (53), NTP (123), and AWS instance metadata (169.254.169.254) remain unrestricted as required substrate.
1943: 
1944: Verify: `curl https://polaris.<domain>/transparency/policy` returns the `egress_allowlist` array and the `enforcement_layer` list (host iptables + Docker DOCKER-USER + AWS Security Group).
1945: 
1946: ## 5. Code provenance
1947: 
1948: `/transparency` returns:
1949: - **`git_commit`** — short SHA of the POLARIS commit deployed
1950: - **`polaris_version`** — `__version__` from `src/polaris_v6/__init__.py`
1951: - **`deploy_timestamp`** — server-side UTC timestamp at /transparency request
1952: - **`region`** — AWS region (`ca-central-1` for Carney demo)
1953: - **`dependencies.python`** — first 50 lines of `requirements-v6.txt`
1954: 
1955: The container images are built from `Dockerfile.v6` and `web/Dockerfile` at the pinned commit; no pre-built registry images are used. Operator can verify by SSM into the host and running `git rev-parse HEAD` inside `/opt/polaris`.
1956: 
1957: ## 6. Filing a sovereignty escalation
1958: 
1959: If a reviewer finds:
1960: - A non-T1 source content surfacing in a verified sentence
1961: - A bundle whose `manifest.yaml.asc` fails GPG verification
1962: - An off-allowlist egress drop in the kernel log (`journalctl -k | grep POLARIS-EGRESS-DROP`)
1963: 
1964: Email the operator with the `run_id` + the specific claim/path. The operator can re-run the bundle generation OR confirm the issue is a Tier-2 source mistakenly marked T1.
1965: 
1966: For the Carney demo, escalation contact is the POLARIS deploy operator listed in the Route 53 zone WHOIS record for the deploy domain.
1967: 
1968: 
1969:  succeeded in 1104ms:
1970: # I-carney-002 — AWS Canada Central deploy
1971: 
1972: Terraform module that provisions the Carney demo on AWS ca-central-1 (Montréal). Hosts the `docker-compose.v6.yml` stack from I-carney-005.
1973: 
1974: ## Prereqs
1975: 
1976: - Terraform >= 1.6.0
1977: - AWS CLI configured (`aws sts get-caller-identity` resolves)
1978: - Pre-existing Route 53 public hosted zone for `<domain_name>`
1979: - IAM permissions: VPC + EC2 + ELB + ACM + Route 53 + KMS + S3 + SSM + IAM + WAF + Backup
1980: - An S3 bucket for Terraform state (created out-of-band; bootstrap one with
1981:   `aws s3 mb s3://polaris-carney-tf-state --region ca-central-1` then
1982:   `aws s3api put-bucket-versioning --bucket polaris-carney-tf-state --versioning-configuration Status=Enabled`)
1983: - The Carney demo GPG signing key has been generated via
1984:   `scripts/bootstrap_gpg_demo_key.sh` from the I-carney-005 substrate;
1985:   you have `state/polaris_gpg_keyid.txt` and `outputs/polaris_demo_pubkey.asc`
1986: 
1987: ## Step 1 — `terraform init`
1988: 
1989: ```bash
1990: cd infra/aws
1991: terraform init \
1992:     -backend-config="bucket=polaris-carney-tf-state" \
1993:     -backend-config="region=ca-central-1" \
1994:     -backend-config="key=polaris-carney/terraform.tfstate"
1995: ```
1996: 
1997: ## Step 2 — fill in tfvars
1998: 
1999: ```bash
2000: cp terraform.tfvars.example terraform.tfvars
2001: # Edit terraform.tfvars: set domain_name, route53_zone_name, secrets,
2002: # polaris_repo_commit (current polaris HEAD SHA), polaris_gpg_key_id,
2003: # polaris_gpg_pubkey (paste from outputs/polaris_demo_pubkey.asc).
2004: ```
2005: 
2006: `terraform.tfvars` is gitignored via the repo-root `.gitignore` — never commit it.
2007: 
2008: ## Step 3 — `terraform plan` then `terraform apply`
2009: 
2010: ```bash
2011: terraform plan -out plan.bin
2012: terraform apply plan.bin
2013: ```
2014: 
2015: Takes ~10 minutes. Watch for:
2016: - VPC + NAT (~3 min)
2017: - ACM cert + DNS validation (~3 min for Route 53 propagation)
2018: - EC2 boot + cloud-init (~5 min — docker pulls + compose build)
2019: 
2020: ## Step 4 — smoke test
2021: 
2022: ```bash
2023: # Public endpoint:
2024: curl -fsS https://polaris.<your-domain>/health
2025: 
2026: # Webui:
2027: curl -fsS https://polaris.<your-domain>/
2028: 
2029: # Submit a run:
2030: curl -fsS -X POST https://polaris.<your-domain>/api/v6/runs \
2031:     -H 'content-type: application/json' \
2032:     -d '{"template":"clinical","question":"smoke test"}'
2033: 
2034: # SSH-via-SSM into the host:
2035: $(terraform output -raw ssm_start_session_command)
2036: ```
2037: 
2038: ## Private GPG key transfer (manual; one-time)
2039: 
2040: Cloud-init imports only the public key into the EC2 GNUPGHOME. The private key signing capability requires you to transfer the secret key from your operator workstation:
2041: 
2042: ```bash
2043: # On operator workstation:
2044: gpg --homedir ~/.gnupg-polaris --armor --export-secret-key "<fingerprint>" > /tmp/polaris-secret.asc
2045: 
2046: # Open an SSM session to the EC2:
2047: aws ssm start-session --target $(terraform output -raw ec2_instance_id) --region ca-central-1
2048: 
2049: # On the EC2 host:
2050: sudo cat > /tmp/polaris-secret.asc <<EOF
2051: <paste the armored secret key here>
2052: EOF
2053: sudo GNUPGHOME=/var/lib/polaris/gpg gpg --import /tmp/polaris-secret.asc
2054: sudo shred -u /tmp/polaris-secret.asc
2055: 
2056: # Restart the api + worker so the GPG signer picks up the now-present
2057: # private key:
2058: cd /opt/polaris
2059: sudo docker compose -f docker-compose.v6.yml restart api worker
2060: ```
2061: 
2062: This is intentionally manual — burning the private key into Terraform / SSM SecureString would put it inside CloudTrail decryption logs and create a longer audit trail than the demo requires.
2063: 
2064: ## Rollback
2065: 
2066: ```bash
2067: terraform destroy
2068: ```
2069: 
2070: WARN: destroys VPC + EC2 + EBS + S3 audit bucket (with all bundles). EBS snapshots in AWS Backup survive `terraform destroy` (separately governed by the backup vault retention) but the vault itself is also torn down — recover snapshots BEFORE destroy if needed.
2071:

2114: import type { NextConfig } from "next";
2115: 
2116: // I-carney-005 P1-004 + P1-005: standalone output for Docker runner +
2117: // server-side rewrite of /api/v6/* → ${INTERNAL_API_URL}/* so browser
2118: // fetch only talks to the webui container (Next.js then proxies to the
2119: // api container by Docker service name, which the browser can't resolve).
2120: const nextConfig: NextConfig = {
2121:   output: "standalone",
2122:   async rewrites() {
2123:     const internal =
2124:       process.env.INTERNAL_API_URL ||
2125:       process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL ||
2126:       "http://localhost:8000";
2127:     return [
2128:       {
2129:         source: "/api/v6/:path*",
2130:         destination: `${internal}/:path*`,
2131:       },
2132:       // I-carney-003 P1-001: expose FastAPI /transparency directly so
2133:       // reviewers (incl. Carney's office) can audit the deploy without
2134:       // going through /api/v6 prefix routing.
2135:       {
2136:         source: "/transparency",
2137:         destination: `${internal}/transparency`,
2138:       },
2139:       {
2140:         source: "/transparency/:path*",
2141:         destination: `${internal}/transparency/:path*`,
2142:       },
2143:     ];
2144:   },
2145: };
2146:

2148:

2500:     # I-carney-004 LAW II: fail-loud at app construction if auth substrate

2540:     # Inject the real Serper + Semantic-Scholar fetcher when keys are
2541:     # present; otherwise leave the dependency at the sentinel default
2542:     # (returns 400 fetch_backend_unavailable, never silently degrades).
2543:     if os.environ.get("SERPER_API_KEY", "").strip():
2544:         from polaris_graph.retrieval2.real_fetcher import build_real_fetcher
2545: 
2546:         # Build once at startup; fetcher reuses httpx connections per call.
2547:         _real_fetcher = build_real_fetcher()
2548: 
2549:         def _inject_real_fetcher():
2550:             return _real_fetcher
2551: 
2552:         app.dependency_overrides[slice002_get_fetch_fn] = _inject_real_fetcher
2553:     app.include_router(slice002_retrieval_router, prefix="/api")
2554: 
2555:     # Slice 003 — POST /api/generation + GET /api/generation/health.
2556:     # When OPENROUTER_API_KEY is present, inject the real OpenRouter-backed
2557:     # completion_fn (PR 7); otherwise leave the sentinel default in place
2558:     # which returns 400 completion_backend_unavailable per LAW II.

2560:         from polaris_graph.generator2.real_completion import build_real_completion
2561: 
2562:         _real_completion = build_real_completion()
2563: 
2564:         def _inject_real_completion():
2565:             return _real_completion
2566:

2568:     app.include_router(slice003_generation_router, prefix="/api")
2569:

2571:     # When POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID is set, build the real GPGSigner and inject
2572:     # it via dep override; otherwise leave sentinel default which yields
2573:     # HTTP 503 (LAW II fail-loud — never ship unsigned bundles).
2574:     if os.environ.get("POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID", "").strip():
2575:         from polaris_graph.audit_bundle.gpg_signer import build_gpg_signer
2576:

2580:             return _real_signer.sign

2597:             return _benchmark_root

2600:             _inject_benchmark_root

2615:  succeeded in 1230ms:
2616: """Runs router — POST /runs, GET /runs/{id}.
2617: 
2618: I-phase0-005 (Codex APPROVE iter 4): persist runs to SQLite via
2619: polaris_v6.queue.run_store and enqueue the Dramatiq actor on POST.
2620: Replaces the previous in-memory `_run_table` dict.
2621: """
2622: 
2623: from __future__ import annotations
2624: 
2625: import sqlite3
2626: import uuid
2627: 
2628: from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException
2629: 
2630: from polaris_v6.queue import run_store
2631: from polaris_v6.queue.actors import enqueue_research_run
2632: from polaris_v6.schemas.run_request import RunRequest
2633: from polaris_v6.schemas.run_status import RunStatusResponse
2634: 
2635: router = APIRouter(prefix="/runs", tags=["runs"])
2636: 
2637: 
2638: @router.post("", response_model=RunStatusResponse, status_code=202)
2639: def create_run(payload: RunRequest) -> RunStatusResponse:
2640:     run_id = uuid.uuid4().hex
2641:     try:
2642:         run_store.insert_run(run_id, payload.template, payload.question)
2643:     except sqlite3.IntegrityError as exc:
2644:         # uuid4 collision is theoretically impossible but defensive
2645:         raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail=f"run {run_id!r} already exists") from exc
2646:     except RuntimeError as exc:
2647:         # init_db permission-denied or similar
2648:         raise HTTPException(status_code=503, detail=str(exc)) from exc
2649: 
2650:     enqueue_research_run.send(run_id, payload.model_dump())
2651: 
2652:     record = run_store.get_run(run_id)
2653:     if record is None:  # pragma: no cover — insert just succeeded
2654:         raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="run row vanished after insert")
2655:     return record
2656: 
2657: 
2658: @router.get("/{run_id}", response_model=RunStatusResponse)
2659: def get_run(run_id: str) -> RunStatusResponse:
2660:     record = run_store.get_run(run_id)
2661:     if record is None:
2662:         raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"run {run_id!r} not found")
2663:     return record
2664: 
2665: 
2666:  succeeded in 1277ms:
2667: """I-carney-004 — static-accounts auth + HS256 JWT.
2668: 
2669: Pattern (Carney-demo scope):
2670: - Operator-curated YAML at `${POLARIS_STATIC_ACCOUNTS_PATH:-/app/config/static_accounts.yaml}`
2671:   with bcrypt-hashed passwords + role.
2672: - POST /auth/login → 12-hour HS256 JWT signed with POLARIS_JWT_SECRET.
2673: - `require_auth` FastAPI dependency injects on all routes except an allowlist
2674:   (/health, /transparency*, /auth/login).
2675: 
2676: LAW II: missing POLARIS_JWT_SECRET at app startup → fail-loud RuntimeError.
2677: Never silently allow unauthenticated traffic.
2678: 
2679: Phase-2: replace with Cognito/Okta when the demo gives way to long-term use.
2680: """
2681: 
2682: from __future__ import annotations
2683: 
2684: import os
2685: import secrets
2686: from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
2687: from pathlib import Path
2688: from typing import Any
2689: 
2690: import yaml
2691: from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, Request
2692: from fastapi.security import HTTPAuthorizationCredentials, HTTPBearer
2693: from jose import JWTError, jwt
2694: from passlib.context import CryptContext
2695: from pydantic import BaseModel
2696: 
2697: JWT_ALGORITHM = "HS256"
2698: JWT_EXPIRY_HOURS = 12
2699: 
2700: # Paths exempt from auth — health probes + reviewer-visible transparency.
2701: PUBLIC_PATH_PREFIXES: tuple[str, ...] = (
2702:     "/health",
2703:     "/transparency",
2704:     "/auth/login",
2705:     "/docs",       # FastAPI's own OpenAPI UI
2706:     "/redoc",
2707:     "/openapi.json",
2708: )
2709: 
2710: DEFAULT_ACCOUNTS_PATH = "/app/config/static_accounts.yaml"
2711: 
2712: _pwd_ctx = CryptContext(schemes=["bcrypt"], deprecated="auto")
2713: _bearer_scheme = HTTPBearer(auto_error=False)
2714: router = APIRouter(tags=["auth"])
2715: 
2716: 
2717: class LoginRequest(BaseModel):
2718:     username: str
2719:     password: str
2720: 
2721: 
2722: class LoginResponse(BaseModel):
2723:     access_token: str
2724:     token_type: str = "bearer"
2725:     role: str
2726:     expires_in: int  # seconds
2727: 
2728: 
2729: class User(BaseModel):
2730:     username: str
2731:     role: str
2732: 
2733: 
2734: def _load_accounts() -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
2735:     """Read the static-accounts YAML. Raises RuntimeError if missing.
2736: 
2737:     LAW II: never silently default to an empty account list (which would
2738:     behave as 'allow nobody' but the operator would not realize the file
2739:     was missing).
2740:     """
2741:     path = Path(os.environ.get("POLARIS_STATIC_ACCOUNTS_PATH", DEFAULT_ACCOUNTS_PATH))
2742:     if not path.exists():
2743:         raise RuntimeError(
2744:             f"static_accounts.yaml not found at {path}. Set "
2745:             "POLARIS_STATIC_ACCOUNTS_PATH or mount the file. The application "
2746:             "refuses to start without the auth substrate (LAW II)."
2747:         )
2748:     body = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
2749:     if not isinstance(body, dict) or not isinstance(body.get("accounts"), list):
2750:         raise RuntimeError(f"static_accounts.yaml at {path} must have top-level 'accounts' list")
2751:     return body["accounts"]
2752: 
2753: 
2754: def _jwt_secret() -> str:
2755:     secret = os.environ.get("POLARIS_JWT_SECRET", "").strip()
2756:     if not secret or len(secret) < 32:
2757:         raise RuntimeError(
2758:             "POLARIS_JWT_SECRET env var missing or too short (<32 chars). "
2759:             "Application refuses to issue tokens with a weak/missing key (LAW II). "
2760:             "Set via AWS Secrets Manager → cloud-init → .env per I-carney-004."
2761:         )
2762:     return secret
2763: 
2764: 
2765: def verify_app_startup() -> None:
2766:     """Called at FastAPI app create_app() time. Fail loud if auth substrate
2767:     is misconfigured. Per LAW II, we never start an auth-gated app with a
2768:     broken auth gate.
2769: 
2770:     Skipped when POLARIS_AUTH_DISABLED=1 (Phase-0 demo + tests).
2771:     """
2772:     if os.environ.get("POLARIS_AUTH_DISABLED", "") == "1":
2773:         return
2774:     _jwt_secret()
2775:     _load_accounts()  # raises if YAML missing or malformed
2776: 
2777: 
2778: def issue_token(username: str, role: str) -> tuple[str, int]:
2779:     """Returns (jwt, expires_in_seconds)."""
2780:     expires_in = JWT_EXPIRY_HOURS * 3600
2781:     payload = {
2782:         "sub": username,
2783:         "role": role,
2784:         "iat": datetime.now(timezone.utc),
2785:         "exp": datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(hours=JWT_EXPIRY_HOURS),
2786:     }
2787:     token = jwt.encode(payload, _jwt_secret(), algorithm=JWT_ALGORITHM)
2788:     return token, expires_in
2789: 
2790: 
2791: def _path_is_public(path: str) -> bool:
2792:     return any(path.startswith(prefix) for prefix in PUBLIC_PATH_PREFIXES)
2793: 
2794: 
2795: async def require_auth(
2796:     request: Request,
2797:     creds: HTTPAuthorizationCredentials | None = Depends(_bearer_scheme),
2798: ) -> User | None:
2799:     """Global FastAPI dependency. None on public paths; raises 401 otherwise.
2800: 
2801:     Phase-0 demo: POLARIS_AUTH_DISABLED=1 short-circuits to None (auth off).
2802:     """
2803:     if os.environ.get("POLARIS_AUTH_DISABLED", "") == "1":
2804:         return None
2805:     if _path_is_public(request.url.path):
2806:         return None
2807:     if creds is None or creds.scheme.lower() != "bearer":
2808:         raise HTTPException(
2809:             status_code=401,
2810:             detail={"error": "missing_bearer_token", "message": "Authorization: Bearer <jwt> required"},
2811:         )
2812:     try:
2813:         payload = jwt.decode(creds.credentials, _jwt_secret(), algorithms=[JWT_ALGORITHM])
2814:     except JWTError as exc:
2815:         raise HTTPException(
2816:             status_code=401,
2817:             detail={"error": "invalid_token", "message": str(exc)},
2818:         ) from exc
2819:     return User(username=payload["sub"], role=payload.get("role", "reviewer"))
2820: 
2821: 
2822: @router.post("/auth/login", response_model=LoginResponse)
2823: def login(payload: LoginRequest) -> LoginResponse:
2824:     accounts = _load_accounts()
2825:     for account in accounts:
2826:         if account.get("username") == payload.username:
2827:             hashed = account.get("password_bcrypt", "")
2828:             if hashed and _pwd_ctx.verify(payload.password, hashed):
2829:                 token, exp_in = issue_token(payload.username, account.get("role", "reviewer"))
2830:                 return LoginResponse(
2831:                     access_token=token,
2832:                     role=account.get("role", "reviewer"),
2833:                     expires_in=exp_in,
2834:                 )
2835:             break
2836:     # Constant-time-ish failure shape (don't leak whether username exists).
2837:     raise HTTPException(
2838:         status_code=401,
2839:         detail={"error": "invalid_credentials"},
2840:     )
2841: 
2842: 
2843: def generate_strong_secret(n_bytes: int = 48) -> str:
2844:     """Helper for operator scripts: 64-char URL-safe random secret."""
2845:     return secrets.token_urlsafe(n_bytes)
2846:

3158: #!/bin/bash
3159: # I-carney-002 — EC2 user-data executed on first boot.
3160: #
3161: # Brings the docker compose v6 stack online on a fresh Ubuntu 24.04 host.
3162: # Idempotent on reboot (`docker compose up -d` is idempotent; SSM fetches
3163: # overwrite .env each boot).
3164: 
3165: # Codex iter-1 P1-3: do NOT enable xtrace globally because it would print
3166: # POLARIS_JWT_SECRET, POLARIS_STATIC_ACCOUNTS_YAML, and POLARIS_GPG_PRIVKEY
3167: # verbatim to cloud-init logs / journald. Errors-only + pipefail.
3168: set -eo pipefail
3169: 
3170: AWS_REGION="${aws_region}"
3171: POLARIS_REPO_URL="${polaris_repo_url}"
3172: POLARIS_REPO_BRANCH="${polaris_repo_branch}"
3173: POLARIS_REPO_COMMIT="${polaris_repo_commit}"
3174: AUDIT_BUCKET_NAME="${audit_bucket_name}"
3175: 
3176: # ----- 1. apt + docker compose v2 + git + awscli -----
3177: export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
3178: apt-get update
3179: apt-get install -y \
3180:     docker.io \
3181:     docker-compose-v2 \
3182:     git \
3183:     awscli \
3184:     gnupg \
3185:     curl \
3186:     jq
3187: 
3188: systemctl enable --now docker
3189: 
3190: # ----- 2. mount /var/lib/polaris on the attached data EBS -----
3191: # Codex diff iter-1 P2 + iter-2 P2: never unconditionally `mkfs -F`; tolerate
3192: # slow EBS attachment by polling for the device for up to 120s. Resolve by
3193: # device-name across instance generations (nvme* on Nitro, xvd* on Xen).
3194: DATA_DEV=""
3195: for attempt in $(seq 1 60); do
3196:     for candidate in /dev/nvme1n1 /dev/xvdf /dev/sdf; do
3197:         if [ -b "$candidate" ]; then
3198:             DATA_DEV="$candidate"
3199:             break 2
3200:         fi
3201:     done
3202:     echo "[cloud-init] waiting for data EBS to attach (attempt $attempt/60)"
3203:     sleep 2
3204: done
3205: if [ -z "$DATA_DEV" ]; then
3206:     echo "[cloud-init] ERROR: data EBS device not attached after 120s — failing loud per LAW II" >&2
3207:     exit 1
3208: fi
3209: echo "[cloud-init] data EBS detected at $DATA_DEV"
3210: 
3211: # Only mkfs if the device has NO existing filesystem (idempotent on replacement).
3212: if ! blkid "$DATA_DEV" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
3213:     echo "[cloud-init] new data volume — formatting $DATA_DEV with ext4"
3214:     mkfs.ext4 -L polaris-data "$DATA_DEV"
3215: else
3216:     echo "[cloud-init] existing filesystem on $DATA_DEV — skipping mkfs"
3217: fi
3218: 
3219: mkdir -p /var/lib/polaris
3220: mount LABEL=polaris-data /var/lib/polaris || mount "$DATA_DEV" /var/lib/polaris
3221: grep -q "polaris-data" /etc/fstab || \
3222:     echo "LABEL=polaris-data /var/lib/polaris ext4 defaults,nofail,noatime 0 2" >> /etc/fstab
3223: 
3224: mkdir -p /var/lib/polaris/{outputs,logs,data,state,gpg}
3225: chmod 700 /var/lib/polaris/gpg
3226: 
3227: # ----- 3. clone POLARIS at pinned commit -----
3228: mkdir -p /opt
3229: if [ ! -d /opt/polaris/.git ]; then
3230:     git clone "$POLARIS_REPO_URL" /opt/polaris
3231: fi
3232: cd /opt/polaris
3233: git fetch origin "$POLARIS_REPO_BRANCH"
3234: git checkout "$POLARIS_REPO_COMMIT"
3235: 
3236: # ----- 4. fetch SSM secrets → /opt/polaris/.env -----
3237: ssm_get() {
3238:     aws ssm get-parameter \
3239:         --name "$1" \
3240:         --with-decryption \
3241:         --region "$AWS_REGION" \
3242:         --query Parameter.Value \
3243:         --output text 2>/dev/null
3244: }
3245: 
3246: OPENROUTER_API_KEY=$(ssm_get /polaris/v6/openrouter_api_key)
3247: SERPER_API_KEY=$(ssm_get /polaris/v6/serper_api_key)
3248: SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY=$(ssm_get /polaris/v6/semantic_scholar_api_key || true)
3249: POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID=$(ssm_get /polaris/v6/polaris_gpg_key_id)
3250: POLARIS_GPG_PUBKEY=$(ssm_get /polaris/v6/polaris_gpg_pubkey || true)
3251: 
3252: # ----- 4b. I-carney-004: fetch Secrets Manager substrate -----
3253: sm_get() {
3254:     aws secretsmanager get-secret-value \
3255:         --secret-id "$1" \
3256:         --region "$AWS_REGION" \
3257:         --query SecretString \
3258:         --output text 2>/dev/null
3259: }
3260: 
3261: POLARIS_JWT_SECRET=$(sm_get polaris/v6/jwt_secret)
3262: POLARIS_STATIC_ACCOUNTS_YAML=$(sm_get polaris/v6/static_accounts_yaml)
3263: POLARIS_GPG_PRIVKEY=$(sm_get polaris/v6/gpg_private_key_armored)
3264: 
3265: # Write static_accounts.yaml to /etc/polaris/ (host) AND make it available
3266: # to the api container via bind-mount. The container path /app/config/
3267: # is baked in by Dockerfile.v6 `COPY config/`; for the runtime override
3268: # we set POLARIS_STATIC_ACCOUNTS_PATH=/etc/polaris/static_accounts.yaml
3269: # in the api+worker env_file so the container reads the host-managed YAML.
3270: mkdir -p /etc/polaris
3271: chmod 750 /etc/polaris
3272: echo "$POLARIS_STATIC_ACCOUNTS_YAML" > /etc/polaris/static_accounts.yaml
3273: chmod 640 /etc/polaris/static_accounts.yaml
3274: 
3275: cat > /opt/polaris/.env <<EOF
3276: OPENROUTER_API_KEY=$OPENROUTER_API_KEY
3277: SERPER_API_KEY=$SERPER_API_KEY
3278: SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY=$SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY
3279: POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID=$POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID
3280: POLARIS_GPG_HOMEDIR=/var/lib/polaris/gpg
3281: POLARIS_API_PORT=8000
3282: POLARIS_WEB_PORT=3000
3283: PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN=5.00
3284: POLARIS_AUDIT_S3_BUCKET=$AUDIT_BUCKET_NAME
3285: POLARIS_GIT_COMMIT=$POLARIS_REPO_COMMIT
3286: AWS_REGION=$AWS_REGION
3287: POLARIS_JWT_SECRET=$POLARIS_JWT_SECRET
3288: POLARIS_STATIC_ACCOUNTS_PATH=/etc/polaris/static_accounts.yaml
3289: EOF
3290: chmod 600 /opt/polaris/.env
3291: 
3292: # ----- 5. import the published GPG public key into the demo keyring -----
3293: # I-carney-002 P1-pending: only the public key is published via SSM; the
3294: # private key MUST be generated/imported by operator out-of-band (the
3295: # bootstrap script runs on the operator workstation per the I-carney-005
3296: # runbook, then the operator copies the private key into the EC2 keyring
3297: # via SSM Session Manager). Cloud-init only imports the public half so
3298: # `bundle.tar.gz` requests will return 503 gpg_unavailable until the
3299: # operator finishes the private-key transfer.
3300: #
3301: # I-carney-003 P1-002: ALSO persist the armored ASCII to disk so the
3302: # /transparency/pubkey.asc endpoint can serve it without shelling to gpg.
3303: if [ -n "$POLARIS_GPG_PUBKEY" ]; then
3304:     export GNUPGHOME=/var/lib/polaris/gpg
3305:     echo "$POLARIS_GPG_PUBKEY" | gpg --import || true
3306:     echo "$POLARIS_GPG_PUBKEY" > /var/lib/polaris/gpg/polaris_demo_pubkey.asc
3307:     chmod 644 /var/lib/polaris/gpg/polaris_demo_pubkey.asc
3308: fi
3309: 
3310: # I-carney-004: import GPG PRIVATE key from Secrets Manager (replaces the
3311: # manual operator-side SSM Session Manager transfer step from I-carney-002).
3312: # Private key remains in the dedicated /var/lib/polaris/gpg keyring (mode 700).
3313: if [ -n "$POLARIS_GPG_PRIVKEY" ]; then
3314:     export GNUPGHOME=/var/lib/polaris/gpg
3315:     # Codex iter-1 P2: fail loud on import error (private key is the entire
3316:     # reason for the bundle-signing substrate; silent failure would let the
3317:     # deploy come up serving 503s on every /runs/{id}/bundle.tar.gz).
3318:     echo "$POLARIS_GPG_PRIVKEY" | gpg --batch --import
3319:     # Wipe the env var so it doesn't leak into journald via systemd unit logs.
3320:     unset POLARIS_GPG_PRIVKEY
3321: fi
3322: 
3323: # ----- 5b. Install egress allowlist + lockdown (I-carney-003) -----
3324: mkdir -p /etc/polaris
3325: cp /opt/polaris/config/egress_allowlist.txt /etc/polaris/egress_allowlist.txt
3326: chmod 644 /etc/polaris/egress_allowlist.txt
3327: # Note: egress_lockdown.sh is NOT auto-run on boot. Operator runs it AFTER
3328: # `docker compose up -d` succeeds the first time so build-time hosts can
3329: # be tightened. See docs/transparency.md §4.
3330: 
3331: # ----- 6. bring up the compose stack -----
3332: cd /opt/polaris
3333: docker compose -f docker-compose.v6.yml up -d --build
3334: 
3335: # ----- 7. self-test -----
3336: sleep 30
3337: for i in $(seq 1 20); do
3338:     if curl -fsS http://localhost:8000/health > /dev/null; then
3339:         echo "[cloud-init] api healthy at boot+$((i*5))s"
3340:         break
3341:     fi
3342:     sleep 5
3343: done

4892:  succeeded in 1036ms:
4893:    1: # POLARIS v6 deploy runbook (Carney demo)
4894:    2: 
4895:    3: **Scope:** single-host `docker compose` deployment for the Carney demo. Production AWS Canada Central infrastructure is tracked separately in **I-carney-002**.
4896:    4: 
4897:    5: ## Prereqs
4898:    6: 
4899:    7: - Docker Engine + Compose v2 (`docker compose version` returns 2.x)
4900:    8: - GPG installed on host (`gpg --version` returns 2.2+)
4901:    9: - A `.env` file at repo root with at minimum:
4902:   10: 
4903:   11: ```
4904:   12: OPENROUTER_API_KEY=<your-key>
4905:   13: SERPER_API_KEY=<your-key>
4906:   14: SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY=<optional>
4907:   15: POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID=<set after Step 1>
4908:   16: POLARIS_GPG_HOMEDIR=~/.gnupg-polaris
4909:   17: POLARIS_API_PORT=8000
4910:   18: POLARIS_WEB_PORT=3000
4911:   19: PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN=5.00
4912:   20: ```
4913:   21: 
4914:   22: `.env` is gitignored; do NOT commit it. A skeleton lives at `.env.example` (added in I-carney-004).
4915:   23: 
4916:   24: ## Step 1 — Bootstrap the GPG demo signing key
4917:   25: 
4918:   26: ```
4919:   27: bash scripts/bootstrap_gpg_demo_key.sh
4920:   28: ```
4921:   29: 
4922:   30: Idempotent: skips if the `POLARIS Carney Demo` key already exists under `$GNUPGHOME`. On success it prints the fingerprint and writes the public key to `outputs/polaris_demo_pubkey.asc`. Copy the printed `POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID=<fingerprint>` line into your `.env`.
4923:   31: 
4924:   32: ## Step 2 — Bring up the stack
4925:   33: 
4926:   34: ```
4927:   35: docker compose -f docker-compose.v6.yml up -d --build
4928:   36: ```
4929:   37: 
4930:   38: This builds the v6 backend image (`Dockerfile.v6`) and the Next.js webui image (`web/Dockerfile`), then starts four services: `redis`, `api`, `worker`, `webui`.
4931:   39: 
4932:   40: Watch the startup:
4933:   41: 
4934:   42: ```
4935:   43: docker compose -f docker-compose.v6.yml logs -f
4936:   44: ```
4937:   45: 
4938:   46: `api` and `worker` will wait for `redis` healthcheck before they start. Expect `[entrypoint] redis reachable` then `[entrypoint] starting uvicorn` (api) or `starting dramatiq worker` (worker).
4939:   47: 
4940:   48: ## Step 3 — Smoke test
4941:   49: 
4942:   50: ```
4943:   51: # Backend health
4944:   52: curl -fsS http://localhost:8000/health
4945:   53: 
4946:   54: # Webui
4947:   55: curl -fsS http://localhost:3000/
4948:   56: 
4949:   57: # I-carney-004: auth-gated endpoints require a Bearer JWT. Get one first:
4950:   58: TOKEN=$(curl -fsS -X POST http://localhost:8000/auth/login \
4951:   59:     -H 'content-type: application/json' \
4952:   60:     -d '{"username":"carney_office","password":"<your-password>"}' \
4953:   61:     | jq -r .access_token)
4954:   62: 
4955:   63: # Submit a sample run (browser-side fetch lands here via /api/v6/runs)
4956:   64: curl -fsS -X POST http://localhost:8000/runs \
4957:   65:     -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
4958:   66:     -H 'content-type: application/json' \
4959:   67:     -d '{"template":"clinical","question":"Is tirzepatide effective for type 2 diabetes?"}'
4960:   68: ```
4961:   69: 
4962:   70: The response is a `RunStatusResponse` with `run_id` (UUID), `lifecycle_status="queued"`. Within a few seconds the Dramatiq worker picks it up and pipeline-A runs.
4963:   71: 
4964:   72: Stream events:
4965:   73: 
4966:   74: ```
4967:   75: curl -N http://localhost:8000/stream/<run_id>
4968:   76: ```
4969:   77: 
4970:   78: ## Step 4 — Rollback
4971:   79: 
4972:   80: ```
4973:   81: docker compose -f docker-compose.v6.yml down
4974:   82: ```
4975:   83: 
4976:   84: For a clean wipe (drops Redis AOF + sqlite v6_runs.sqlite — DATA LOSS):
4977:   85: 
4978:   86: ```
4979:   87: docker compose -f docker-compose.v6.yml down -v
4980:   88: ```
4981:   89: 
4982:   90: ## Troubleshooting
4983:   91: 
4984:   92: | Symptom | Likely cause | Fix |
4985:   93: |---|---|---|
4986:   94: | `api` keeps restarting; logs show `redis unreachable after 10s` | Redis service hasn't initialized | `docker compose logs redis` — usually first-run AOF setup; wait 30s |
4987:   95: | 503 `gpg_unavailable` from `/runs/{id}/bundle.tar.gz` | `POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID` empty or key missing | Re-run Step 1; verify `docker compose exec api gpg --list-keys` shows the key |
4988:   96: | Webui returns 502 from `/api/v6/*` | Internal proxy can't reach `api` service | `docker compose exec webui wget --spider http://api:8000/health` |
4989:   97: | `state/v6_runs.sqlite` not writable | `shared_state` volume permissions | `docker compose exec api ls -la /app/state`; rebuild volume |
4990:   98: | Pipeline-A 400 `cited_span_unreachable_after_snapshot` | Evidence pool full_text shorter than cited span | Check `outputs/<run_id>/evidence_pool.json` snippet length |
4991:   99: 
4992:  100: For env-var diagnostics:
4993:  101: 
4994:  102: ```
4995:  103: docker compose -f docker-compose.v6.yml exec api /entrypoint.sh preflight
4996:  104: ```
4997:  105: 
4998:  106: This runs `scripts/v6_preflight.py` which checks env vars, redis reachability, GPG keyring, and run_store write perms — exits non-zero with a checklist of failures.

5015: """I-carney-003 — public transparency endpoint.
5016: 
5017: Returns deploy provenance (region, signing key, sovereignty filter coverage,
5018: evaluator models, egress allowlist) so reviewers and Carney's office can
5019: audit that the deploy is sovereign + verifiable.
5020: 
5021: Three routes:
5022: - GET /transparency         JSON deploy descriptor
5023: - GET /transparency/pubkey.asc  text/plain armored GPG public key
5024: - GET /transparency/policy  JSON full sovereignty + egress policy
5025: """
5026: 
5027: from __future__ import annotations
5028: 
5029: import os
5030: import subprocess
5031: from datetime import datetime, timezone
5032: from pathlib import Path
5033: 
5034: from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException
5035: from fastapi.responses import PlainTextResponse
5036: from pydantic import BaseModel
5037: 
5038: from polaris_v6 import __version__
5039: 
5040: router = APIRouter(prefix="/transparency", tags=["transparency"])
5041: 
5042: POLICY_VERSION = "v1.0"
5043: DEFAULT_PUBKEY_PATH = "/app/gpg/polaris_demo_pubkey.asc"
5044: # Codex diff iter-1 P1-004: /etc/polaris/* only exists on the EC2 host (cloud-
5045: # init copies it there). The api container has /app/config/ baked in by
5046: # Dockerfile.v6 via `COPY config/ config/`. Default to the in-container path
5047: # so /transparency returns the real 17-domain allowlist without operator
5048: # bind-mount; the host /etc/polaris path is used by egress_lockdown.sh only.
5049: DEFAULT_EGRESS_ALLOWLIST = "/app/config/egress_allowlist.txt"
5050: 
5051: 
5052: class SovereigntyPolicy(BaseModel):
5053:     """Sovereignty filter description — cited tiers, cascade behavior."""
5054: 
5055:     cleared_tiers: list[str]
5056:     cascade_rule: str
5057:     tier_definitions: dict[str, str]
5058: 
5059: 
5060: class TransparencyResponse(BaseModel):
5061:     """Public deploy descriptor."""
5062: 
5063:     region: str
5064:     git_commit: str
5065:     polaris_version: str
5066:     deploy_timestamp: str
5067:     signing_key_id: str | None
5068:     signing_key_fingerprint: str | None
5069:     sovereignty_filter: SovereigntyPolicy
5070:     evaluator_models: dict[str, str]
5071:     egress_allowlist: list[str]
5072:     dependencies: dict[str, list[str]]
5073: 
5074: 
5075: def _load_egress_allowlist() -> list[str]:
5076:     """Load the in-container egress allowlist or fall back to 'unrestricted'.
5077: 
5078:     Default path is /app/config/egress_allowlist.txt (baked into Dockerfile.v6
5079:     via `COPY config/ config/`); operator override via POLARIS_EGRESS_ALLOWLIST.
5080:     """
5081:     path = Path(os.environ.get("POLARIS_EGRESS_ALLOWLIST", DEFAULT_EGRESS_ALLOWLIST))
5082:     if not path.exists():
5083:         return ["unrestricted (no lockdown applied)"]
5084:     return [
5085:         line.strip()
5086:         for line in path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines()
5087:         if line.strip() and not line.strip().startswith("#")
5088:     ]
5089: 
5090: 
5091: def _load_dependencies() -> dict[str, list[str]]:
5092:     """Best-effort dependency listing for transparency.
5093: 
5094:     Reads requirements-v6.txt if present (backend image bakes this in).
5095:     web/package.json may or may not be present in the backend image —
5096:     fail silently and emit empty list rather than 503 on absence.
5097:     """
5098:     deps: dict[str, list[str]] = {"python": [], "node": []}
5099:     py_req = Path("/app/requirements-v6.txt")
5100:     if py_req.exists():
5101:         deps["python"] = [
5102:             line.strip()
5103:             for line in py_req.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines()
5104:             if line.strip() and not line.strip().startswith("#") and not line.strip().startswith("-")
5105:         ][:50]  # cap to keep response small
5106:     # web/package.json optional — backend image doesn't include it.
5107:     return deps
5108: 
5109: 
5110: def _git_commit() -> str:
5111:     """Resolve git commit SHA in order:
5112: 
5113:     1. POLARIS_GIT_COMMIT env var — injected at image build time (Dockerfile.v6
5114:        arg) or by cloud-init from the pinned commit it checked out. This is
5115:        the production path; Codex diff iter-2 P1 noted that the container
5116:        does NOT have .git because Dockerfile.v6 only COPYs src/scripts/config.
5117:     2. .git/HEAD probe in /opt/polaris or cwd — local-dev path.
5118:     3. "unknown" — fail-soft.
5119:     """
5120:     explicit = os.environ.get("POLARIS_GIT_COMMIT", "").strip()
5121:     if explicit:
5122:         return explicit[:12]
5123:     for candidate in [Path("/opt/polaris"), Path.cwd()]:
5124:         head_file = candidate / ".git" / "HEAD"
5125:         if head_file.exists():
5126:             head = head_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip()
5127:             if head.startswith("ref: "):
5128:                 ref_path = candidate / ".git" / head[5:]
5129:                 if ref_path.exists():
5130:                     return ref_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip()[:12]
5131:             return head[:12]
5132:     return "unknown"
5133: 
5134: 
5135: def _signing_key_info() -> tuple[str | None, str | None]:
5136:     """Read POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID and look up the fingerprint via gpg if available."""
5137:     key_id = os.environ.get("POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID", "").strip() or None
5138:     if not key_id:
5139:         return None, None
5140:     # The env var IS the fingerprint per I-carney-005 convention.
5141:     return key_id, key_id
5142: 
5143: 
5144: def _sovereignty_policy() -> SovereigntyPolicy:
5145:     """Encodes the artifact_to_slice_chain filter constants for reviewers."""
5146:     return SovereigntyPolicy(
5147:         cleared_tiers=["T1"],
5148:         cascade_rule=(
5149:             "Sentences citing non-T1 sources are redacted from the bundle. "
5150:             "Sections with zero passing sentences are dropped. Reports "
5151:             "with zero passing sections raise "
5152:             "SovereigntyFilterEmptiedReportError (HTTP 422)."
5153:         ),
5154:         tier_definitions={
5155:             "T1": "Regulatory + clinical guideline corpora (FDA, EMA, NICE, etc.) — legal-cleared by default",
5156:             "T2": "Peer-reviewed primary literature — requires explicit operator clearance",
5157:             "T3": "Unverified web content + raw uploads — defaults excluded; falls back to T3 on unknown tiers",
5158:         },
5159:     )
5160: 
5161: 
5162: def _read_pubkey() -> str:
5163:     """Read the armored GPG public key, falling back to gpg --export.
5164: 
5165:     Per Codex iter-2 P1-002: cloud-init writes the file alongside the GPG
5166:     import, but local dev may not have the file. Fall back to shelling out
5167:     to gpg if POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID is set. Strict 503 if neither works.
5168:     """
5169:     path = Path(os.environ.get("POLARIS_GPG_PUBKEY_PATH", DEFAULT_PUBKEY_PATH))
5170:     if path.exists():
5171:         return path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
5172:     key_id = os.environ.get("POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID", "").strip()
5173:     if key_id:
5174:         try:
5175:             result = subprocess.run(
5176:                 ["gpg", "--armor", "--export", key_id],
5177:                 capture_output=True,
5178:                 text=True,
5179:                 timeout=10,
5180:                 check=False,
5181:             )
5182:             if result.returncode == 0 and "BEGIN PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK" in result.stdout:
5183:                 return result.stdout
5184:         except Exception:
5185:             pass
5186:     raise HTTPException(
5187:         status_code=503,
5188:         detail={
5189:             "error": "pubkey_unavailable",
5190:             "message": (
5191:                 f"Public key not found at {path} and gpg --export fallback failed. "
5192:                 "Run scripts/bootstrap_gpg_demo_key.sh on the operator workstation "
5193:                 "and confirm cloud-init imported the key."
5194:             ),
5195:         },
5196:     )
5197: 
5198: 
5199: @router.get("", response_model=TransparencyResponse)
5200: def transparency() -> TransparencyResponse:
5201:     """Public deploy descriptor."""
5202:     key_id, fpr = _signing_key_info()
5203:     return TransparencyResponse(
5204:         region=os.environ.get("AWS_REGION", "unknown"),
5205:         git_commit=_git_commit(),
5206:         polaris_version=__version__,
5207:         deploy_timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
5208:         signing_key_id=key_id,
5209:         signing_key_fingerprint=fpr,
5210:         sovereignty_filter=_sovereignty_policy(),
5211:         evaluator_models={
5212:             "generator": os.environ.get("PG_GENERATOR_MODEL", "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro"),
5213:             "evaluator": os.environ.get("PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL", "qwen/qwen-2.5-72b-instruct"),
5214:         },
5215:         egress_allowlist=_load_egress_allowlist(),
5216:         dependencies=_load_dependencies(),
5217:     )
5218: 
5219: 
5220: @router.get("/pubkey.asc", response_class=PlainTextResponse)
5221: def pubkey() -> PlainTextResponse:
5222:     """Armored GPG public key for bundle signature verification."""
5223:     body = _read_pubkey()
5224:     return PlainTextResponse(content=body, media_type="text/plain; charset=us-ascii")
5225:

5227: class PolicyResponse(BaseModel):
5228:     """Full sovereignty + egress policy with version string."""
5229: 
5230:     version: str
5231:     sovereignty_filter: SovereigntyPolicy
5232:     egress_allowlist: list[str]
5233:     enforcement_layer: list[str]
5234: 
5235: 
5236: @router.get("/policy", response_model=PolicyResponse)
5237: def policy() -> PolicyResponse:
5238:     return PolicyResponse(
5239:         version=POLICY_VERSION,
5240:         sovereignty_filter=_sovereignty_policy(),
5241:         egress_allowlist=_load_egress_allowlist(),
5242:         enforcement_layer=[
5243:             "host iptables OUTPUT chain (scripts/egress_lockdown.sh)",
5244:             "Docker DOCKER-USER chain (scripts/egress_lockdown.sh)",
5245:             # Codex diff iter-2 P2: AWS SG on EC2 is NOT an egress allowlist
5246:             # (egress is all-permit per infra/aws/ec2.tf). Listed here as
5247:             # "ingress-only" to be honest about layer scope.
5248:             "AWS Security Group on EC2 (ingress-only; egress all-permit)",
5249:         ],
5250:     )

5325: docs\transparency.md:55:The EC2 host runs `scripts/egress_lockdown.sh` which installs iptables rules in BOTH `OUTPUT` (host-originated) and `DOCKER-USER` (container-forwarded) chains. Off-allowlist traffic on 80/443 is **dropped + logged via the kernel iptables LOG target** with prefix `[POLARIS-EGRESS-DROP]`. The script's own install/config events go to `/var/log/polaris-egress.log`; the drop log lines land wherever rsyslog/journald is configured to forward kernel facility messages (typically `/var/log/kern.log` or `journalctl -k`).

5344:    1: #!/bin/bash
5345:    2: # I-carney-003 — install iptables egress lockdown on the host.
5346:    3: #
5347:    4: # Reads /etc/polaris/egress_allowlist.txt and installs rules in BOTH OUTPUT
5348:    5: # (host) and DOCKER-USER (container forwarded traffic) chains so neither
5349:    6: # host nor containers can reach off-allowlist destinations on 80/443.
5350:    7: #
5351:    8: # Idempotent: flushes the POLARIS_EGRESS_HOST + POLARIS_EGRESS_DOCKER chains
5352:    9: # before reapplying. Per Codex iter-2 P1-003: covers BOTH host outbound
5353:   10: # AND Docker-bridge-forwarded container traffic.
5354:   11: #
5355:   12: # Run AFTER the first `docker compose build` (build-time hosts in
5356:   13: # config/egress_allowlist.txt are deb.debian.org / pypi.org / etc).
5357:   14: 
5358:   15: set -eo pipefail
5359:   16: 
5360:   17: ALLOWLIST_PATH="${POLARIS_EGRESS_ALLOWLIST:-/etc/polaris/egress_allowlist.txt}"
5361:   18: LOG_FILE="${POLARIS_EGRESS_LOG:-/var/log/polaris-egress.log}"
5362:   19: 
5363:   20: if [ ! -f "$ALLOWLIST_PATH" ]; then
5364:   21:     echo "[egress] ERROR: allowlist file not found at $ALLOWLIST_PATH" >&2
5365:   22:     echo "[egress] copy config/egress_allowlist.txt → $ALLOWLIST_PATH first" >&2
5366:   23:     exit 1
5367:   24: fi
5368:   25: 
5369:   26: if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
5370:   27:     echo "[egress] ERROR: must run as root (uses iptables)" >&2
5371:   28:     exit 1
5372:   29: fi
5373:   30: 
5374:   31: log() {
5375:   32:     local ts
5376:   33:     ts=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
5377:   34:     echo "[$ts] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
5378:   35: }
5379:   36: 
5380:   37: # Resolve allowlist domains to unique A records.
5381:   38: resolve_allowlist() {
5382:   39:     local domain ip
5383:   40:     while IFS= read -r domain; do
5384:   41:         # Skip blanks + comments.
5385:   42:         [[ -z "$domain" || "$domain" =~ ^# ]] && continue
5386:   43:         # getent returns "ip canonical alias" lines; awk picks the first column.
5387:   44:         while IFS= read -r ip; do
5388:   45:             [ -n "$ip" ] && echo "$ip"
5389:   46:         done < <(getent ahostsv4 "$domain" 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1}' | sort -u)
5390:   47:     done < "$ALLOWLIST_PATH" | sort -u
5391:   48: }
5392:   49: 
5393:   50: install_chain() {
5394:   51:     local chain_name="$1"
5395:   52:     local parent_chain="$2"
5396:   53:     local resolved="$3"
5397:   54: 
5398:   55:     log "installing chain $chain_name into $parent_chain"
5399:   56: 
5400:   57:     # (Re)create the chain.
5401:   58:     iptables -F "$chain_name" 2>/dev/null || iptables -N "$chain_name"
5402:   59: 
5403:   60:     # Allow loopback + established + related.
5404:   61:     iptables -A "$chain_name" -o lo -j ACCEPT || true
5405:   62:     iptables -A "$chain_name" -m conntrack --ctstate ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT
5406:   63: 
5407:   64:     # Allow DNS (53), NTP (123), AWS instance metadata (169.254.169.254).
5408:   65:     iptables -A "$chain_name" -p udp --dport 53 -j ACCEPT
5409:   66:     iptables -A "$chain_name" -p tcp --dport 53 -j ACCEPT
5410:   67:     iptables -A "$chain_name" -p udp --dport 123 -j ACCEPT
5411:   68:     iptables -A "$chain_name" -d 169.254.169.254/32 -j ACCEPT
5412:   69: 
5413:   70:     # Allow allowlisted IPs on 443 + 80.
5414:   71:     while IFS= read -r ip; do
5415:   72:         [ -z "$ip" ] && continue
5416:   73:         iptables -A "$chain_name" -d "$ip" -p tcp --dport 443 -j ACCEPT
5417:   74:         iptables -A "$chain_name" -d "$ip" -p tcp --dport 80 -j ACCEPT
5418:   75:     done <<< "$resolved"
5419:   76: 
5420:   77:     # Drop everything else on 80/443.
5421:   78:     iptables -A "$chain_name" -p tcp --dport 443 -j LOG --log-prefix "[POLARIS-EGRESS-DROP] "
5422:   79:     iptables -A "$chain_name" -p tcp --dport 443 -j DROP
5423:   80:     iptables -A "$chain_name" -p tcp --dport 80 -j LOG --log-prefix "[POLARIS-EGRESS-DROP] "
5424:

5578: .\outputs\codex_findings\dr_output_pass_1\codex_stderr.log:1675:    "https://pure.johnshopkins.edu/en/publications/tirzepatide-and-health-related-quality-of-life-in-adults-with-obe/",

5582: .\outputs\codex_findings\dr_output_pass_1\codex_stderr.log:2987:outputs\polaris_graph\GEMINI_E2E_20260316_092353.json:44586:  "draft_report": "# Comparative Analysis of Adhesion Testing Methods for Polymer Coatings: Methodologies, Performance, and Regulatory Compliance\n\n## Abstract\n\nThis report evaluates adhesion testing methodologies for polymer coatings, specifically comparing pull-off, cross-cut, and peel tests across epoxy, urethane, and silicone materials. The scope encompasses methodological standards, failure analysis, and environmental impacts on bond integrity, drawing from established ASTM and ISO protocols. Exclusions involve non-polymer substrates, dynamic fatigue testing not covered by static adhesion standards, and proprietary industrial methods lacking public documentation. Evidence limitations include variability in industry-specific documentation and potential gaps in recent silicone-specific comparative data regarding long-term exposure. The analysis synthesizes regulatory requirements with practical performance metrics to guide method selection. Findings highlight critical distinctions in failure mode classification and the influence of surface preparation on test validity. Regulatory compliance and warranty documentation requirements are also addressed to ensure operational reliability. This structured comparison aims to reduce testing ambiguity and improve consistency in coating performance evaluation across diverse service conditions. Furthermore, economic implications of testing choices are considered to balance accuracy with operational costs. The report concludes with strategic recommendations for optimizing test selection based on material properties. Overall, the study provides a comprehensive framework for stakeholders seeking to validate coating durability and adherence quality in complex engineering applications.\n\n## Standardized Test Method Definitions and ASTM ISO Frameworks\n\nAdhesion testing frameworks for polymer coatings are governed by a complex array of ASTM and ISO standards that define methodological rigor and compliance thresholds [CITE:ev_057ad7d7fadf39c2]. These standardized protocols establish the baseline for comparing pull-off, cross-cut, and peel tests across epoxy, urethane, and silicone materials [CITE:ev_2b7ca35715831266]. The regulatory landscape distinguishes between quantitative tensile measurements and qualitative visual assessments, each serving specific diagnostic purposes within the coating lifecycle [CITE:ev_84a08742318ed15d]. Pull-off adhesion testing applies tensile force perpendicular to the substrate to measure coating resistance to separation [CITE:ev_057ad7d7fadf39c2]. This method is utilized to gage adhesive strength of the coating once applied and cured as per manufacturer's guidelines [CITE:ev_2b7ca35715831266]. The pull-off adhesion tester measures adhesion of coatings to metal, wood, concrete, and rigid substrates, providing versatility across material classes [CITE:ev_0a2f7e8a1aa2c852]. Pull-off test data provides quantitative indication of bond tensile strength [CITE:ev_448284e198da9b99]. Industrial-grade adhesives typically have tensile strength ranging from 10 MPa to over 60 MPa depending on formulation [CITE:ev_a85f361a2bdff124]. Hydroxyapatite coatings showed repeatable adhesion strength of 3.4 \u00b1 1.0 MPa across both ASTM test methods [CITE:ev_5bd33c7a02d9cd28]. ASTM C633-01 test uses 25 mm diameter cylindrical blocks with 38 mm length for adhesion testing [CITE:ev_e9f86ec169988ea]. ASTM D4541 and BS EN ISO 4624 describe pull-off adhesion testing standards for protective coatings [CITE:ev_befc6654b3c8f5ad]. The pull-off test is standardized by ISO 4624:2023 and is widely used to quantify adhesion [CITE:ev_7267f03f92de9267]. ASTM D7234 defines the standard test method for pull-off adhesion strength on concrete using portable testers [CITE:ev_3a7d481c74614432]. Pull-off testing follows ISO 4624, ASTM D4541, and D7234 standards for evaluating coating peel strength [CITE:ev_0ffc84130522e5ce]. Valid pull-off test requires coating to cover at least 50% of stamp surface after detachment [CITE:ev_d0994f38fa60bebe].\n\nThe Cross-Cut Test provides qualitative evaluation while the Pull-Off Method provides quantitative evaluation of coating adhesion [CITE:ev_84a08742318ed15d]. This distinction is critical when selecting methods for epoxy, urethane, or silicone systems, as quantitative data may be required for structural warranty validation. Cross-Cut Test classifies adhesion results into six levels according to the degree of peeling after tape removal [CITE:ev_cd6ffc422440dd18]. The procedure involves making lattice-pattern cuts through the coating to the substrate, applying adhesive tape, and peeling in one motion [CITE:ev_4add28fe26ddd5e3]. ASTM D3359 outlines standardized test methods for evaluating adhesion of coatings to metallic substrates using pressure-sensitive tape [CITE:ev_815bc543dbac8f04]. This standard is widely recognized for evaluating coating adhesion on metal substrates using the tape test method [CITE:ev_59cf9ed236eb49bd]. ISO 2409 specifies the cross-cut test method for evaluating adhesion of coatings on sheet metal parts [CITE:ev_067cc0abd42529d2]. ASTM D3359 defines adhesion as molecular attraction and mechanical bonds between coating and substrate [CITE:ev_a930d76424157b92]. ASTM D3359 procedures assess the adhesion of coating films specifically on metallic substrates [CITE:ev_949116df55b626a8]. The tape test involves applying and removing pressure-sensitive tape over cuts made in the film [CITE:ev_b737d3f89fc71af0]. The tape test is qualitative and cannot precisely assess quantitatively the actual strength of the adhesion [CITE:ev_389d441cc3fe3ba4]. Cross-Cut Test uses pressure-sensitive tape spread over cuts and rapidly pulled off [CITE:ev_25929b033d8bd227]. Adhesion is rated by inspecting the cut area for coating removal relative to classifications 0B through 5B [CITE:ev_d23f5ac9162865a3]. Cross-cut adhesion test evaluation ranges from 0B representing complete detachment to 5B representing no detachment [CITE:ev_d0d69c4b450fa1e3]. ASTM D3359 Standard Test Method for Measuring Adhesion by Tape Test was codified in 1974 [CITE:ev_cee97c994fefc9b1]. ASTM D3359 is a standard test method for measuring adhesion by tape test [CITE:ev_23c66a42fadbecc5]. Crosshatch adhesion testing follows ASTM D3359 standard with grid cuts through coating [CITE:ev_2365cdb310fc8adf]. ASTM D3359 is the standard method for rating adhesion by tape test [CITE:ev_d2c3f9dace34682e].\n\nPeel testing represents a third category, measuring the force required to strip a flexible coating from a substrate [CITE:ev_6e9c26b6c7722bbe]. Peel tests measure adhesive strength by pulling one material away from another at a specific angle and speed [CITE:ev_f0f1594bc5d9dd20]. This method is suitable for designs where bonded edges experience prying or bending [CITE:ev_1be768df70c46a07]. Peel and adhesion strength testing measures bond strength when force attempts separation [CITE:ev_27f9ac91a5f22e9b]. Comprehensive adhesive suitability requires comparing peel adhesion, tensile strength, and shear resistance [CITE:ev_ac671c6a322fe8bd]. The peel test is a method used in materials science to measure the bond strength between two materials [CITE:ev_6bcf28f6b901e894]. Peel strength is usually reported in units of force per width such as N/mm or lb/in [CITE:ev_44fea46094d83d2a]. Peel testing methods include T-peel and 90-degree peel tests standardized under ASTM D1876 and D6862 [CITE:ev_16c2d5e21aa178ca]. Standard testing procedures evaluate peel force under constant temperature 23\u00b11\u00b0C and 50\u00b15% RH [CITE:ev_4420310a01914e20]. Peel test values represent an average between 5 and 35mm of peel for each of two tests [CITE:ev_e84426d0da5ec986]. T-peel testing was performed according to BS 5350-C12:1994/ISO 11339:1993 standard on aluminium alloy [CITE:ev_f93ca08a9dc0966f]. Chinese national standard GB/T 2790-1995 specifies the 180\u00b0 peel strength test method steps and requirements [CITE:ev_4ec63e8773cfcd3e]. ISO 8510-2 covers 180-degree peel test for flexible-bonded-to-rigid test specimen assembly [CITE:ev_75d316de9669a24a]. The T shaped peel test ASTM D1876 is most widely used for thin-gauge metal adherends [CITE:ev_e3a5ffd4420e583a].\n\nTable 1 summarizes the comparative frameworks for these primary adhesion testing methods, highlighting standard designations and operational limits.\n\n| Test Method | Primary Standard (ASTM/ISO) | Substrate Suitability | Measurement Type | Thickness Limit |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| Pull-Off | ASTM D4541, ISO 4624 | Metal, Concrete, Rigid | Quantitative (MPa) | N/A [CITE:ev_0ffc84130522e5ce] |\n| Cross-Cut | ASTM D3359, ISO 2409 | Metal, Plastic, Rigid | Qualitative (0B-5B) | 250 \u00b5m [CITE:ev_a256fbf9c6062207] |\n| Peel | ASTM D1876, ISO 11339 | Flexible-to-Rigid | Quantitative (N/mm) | N/A [CITE:ev_16c2d5e21aa178ca] |\n| Knife/Scrape | ASTM D6677 | Hard Coatings | Qualitative/Force | N/A [CITE:ev_3f498ca240e7a532] |\n\nThickness constraints significantly influence method selection for polymer coatings. The Cross-Cut Test is limited by coating thickness with a maximum of 250 micrometers [CITE:ev_a256fbf9c6062207]. Cross-cut test is applicable for layer thicknesses up to about 250 \u00b5m [CITE:ev_df003953c7f7a2d0]. This limitation renders the cross-cut method unsuitable for thick urethane or epoxy linings often exceeding this threshold. Conversely, the test method covers determination of practical adhesion strength of hard ceramic coatings on metal and ceramic substrates [CITE:ev_f6c7a8ecd3f37045]. Test method is commonly used for coating thicknesses in the range of 0.1 to 30 micrometers [CITE:ev_8858c4a9dba538b2]. Adhesion pull-off strength testing determines the overall quality and integrity of an installed coating system [CITE:ev_c357968c8f3b130a]. Cross hatch tape testing is intended for laboratory use on coatings under 5 mils thickness [CITE:ev_c8b75c8d91e7faea]. Scrape test adhesion is measured in kilograms of force required for removal [CITE:ev_73f6c6e661c92e30]. ASTM D6677 is the standard method for evaluating adhesion by knife [CITE:ev_3f498ca240e7a532]. Cross-cut test is especially suitable for thin layers on rigid substrates like metal or plastic [CITE:ev_bb2ddf4927283db1]. The crosscut adhesion test is a standard ASTM or ISO procedure for dry coatings [CITE:ev_49557422c8fc215a].\n\nConflicting evidence exists regarding the fundamental capabilities of these tests and specific procedural dimensions. One source states the pull-off adhesion test measures the strength of the bond between a coating and the surface [CITE:ev_d62d99d84a009e00]. However, conflicting evidence indicates the test method does not measure fundamental adhesion strength of the bond between coating and substrate [CITE:ev_1a095db8b38db71d]. This discrepancy suggests a distinction between practical field adhesion and theoretical interfacial strength. Regarding cut patterns, evidence states cross-cut test uses a tool to cut a right-angle lattice pattern through the coating to the substrate [CITE:ev_183b421be46e5e8b]. Conversely, other evidence indicates X-cut tape test uses a sharp tool to make an X-shaped cut through the coating to substrate [CITE:ev_377599bde46462b1]. ASTM D3359 accommodates both via Method A (X-Cut) and Method B (Cross-Cut Tape Test), with Method B preferred for laboratory or controlled environments [CITE:ev_a673f1e5d40af5bb]. Test Method A (X-Cut Tape Test) is designed primarily for field use with X-shaped cut in coating [CITE:ev_e46b804dd9b8226]. Cross-Hatch Test is typically used on witness samples of the same substrate material [CITE:ev_26a79c112c1e2bd7].\n\nProcedural variations also appear in peel test specimen dimensions. Evidence indicates DOWSIL ALLGUARD coating adhesion test uses cheesecloth strip 1 inch by 12 inches embedded in wet coating [CITE:ev_234e921351755d24]. In conflict, another source states peel test uses 1 inch wide by 6 inch long fabric strip embedded in coating base layer [CITE:ev_14c32495c2f3b455]. These variations likely reflect specific manufacturer protocols versus general standardized methods. ISO 4624 is referenced as a global standard for pull-off adhesion testing compliance [CITE:ev_a9eaff2972823a42]. Traditional adhesion tests like ISO/JIS cross-hatch and ASTM tape tests only evaluate final adhesion strength without explaining adhesion mechanisms [CITE:ev_60e99e00ac021116]. Surface free energy and adhesion parameter measurements are proposed as complementary methods to traditional cross-cut tape tests [CITE:ev_d77bb9a950ab7132]. Adhesion testing mechanics are complicated when polymer adhesive and metal substrate have different elastic moduli [CITE:ev_2fc7d6f56363052a]. This complexity is particularly relevant for silicone and urethane coatings which exhibit different elastic properties compared to epoxy systems. Peel and pull-off data should not be compared since testing mechanisms differ [CITE:ev_aabe5f967b113f45]. Four common methods exist for testing adhesion of protective coatings and linings per ASTM standards [CITE:ev_c53e1e15e6c509b0]. Common adhesion measuring techniques are performed using a knife or a pull-off adhesion tester [CITE:ev_ac710f7b1e8e52c6].\n\nSpecific material considerations emerge within the evidence regarding epoxy and silicone systems. Results showed good agreement between hot water-adhesion tests and TxDOT peel tests [CITE:ev_6c07ce38730c09f8]. This suggests environmental conditioning impacts epoxy coating performance assessment.
