This contradiction requires resolution: while pull-off testing provides numerical strength values in MPa or psi revealing the coating's tensile pull-off strength indicating adhesion, these values reflect practical adhesion rather than intrinsic molecular bonding [CITE:ev_11ae17d7fc3f76c6]. ASTM D4541 and BS EN ISO 4624 describe pull-off adhesion testing standards for protective coatings, establishing international consistency for quantitative assessment [CITE:ev_befc6654b3c8f5ad]. ISO 4624 is referenced as a global standard for pull-off adhesion testing compliance, further validating the method for regulatory contexts [CITE:ev_a9eaff2972823a42].\n\nCross-cut testing offers qualitative evaluation contrasting with pull-off quantitative assessment [CITE:ev_84a08742318ed15d]. ASTM D3359 outlines standardized test methods for evaluating adhesion of coatings to metallic substrates using pressure-sensitive tape, with the standard codified in 1974 [CITE:ev_815bc543dbac8f04][CITE:ev_cee97c994fefc9b1]. The cross-cut test classifies adhesion results into six levels according to the degree of peeling after tape removal, providing categorical rather than continuous data [CITE:ev_cd6ffc422440dd18]. ISO 2409 specifies the cross-cut test method for evaluating adhesion of coatings on sheet metal parts, establishing international consistency for this qualitative approach [CITE:ev_067cc0abd42529d2]. JIS K 5600-5-6: 1999 specifies the Cross-Cut Test method for coating adhesion evaluation under Japanese Industrial Standards, further demonstrating global standardization of this method [CITE:ev_38e4b21684c9f29c]. The tape test is qualitative and cannot precisely assess quantitatively the actual strength of the adhesion, limiting its utility for comparing epoxy, urethane, and silicone materials on a continuous scale [CITE:ev_389d441cc3fe3ba4]. Cross hatch tape testing is intended for laboratory use on coatings under 5 mils thickness, restricting field applicability for thicker polymer systems [CITE:ev_c8b75c8d91e7faea].\n\nPeel tests measure adhesive strength by pulling one material away from another at a specific angle and speed, offering a third methodological approach [CITE:ev_f0f1594bc5d9dd20]. Comprehensive adhesive suitability requires comparing peel adhesion, tensile strength, and shear resistance, suggesting no single test method suffices for complete material characterization [CITE:ev_ac671c6a322fe8bd]. Results showed good agreement between hot water-adhesion tests and TxDOT peel tests, indicating peel methods correlate with environmental exposure performance [CITE:ev_6c07ce38730c09f8]. For silicone materials specifically, cure improves adhesion except for silicone, where failure occurred at the tape interface, suggesting peel testing underestimates silicone coating performance [CITE:ev_0db5383239a26b95]. Traditional adhesion tests like ISO/JIS cross-hatch and ASTM tape tests only evaluate final adhesion strength without explaining adhesion mechanisms, limiting diagnostic capability for failure analysis [CITE:ev_60e99e00ac021116]. Surface free energy and adhesion parameter measurements proposed as complementary method to traditional cross-cut tape tests, suggesting contamination assessment requires both traditional testing and surface science approaches [CITE:ev_d77bb9a950ab7132].\n\nFailure mode classification distinguishes between adhesive and cohesive failure, with critical implications for interpreting test results. Adhesive failure indicates molecular attraction between adhesive and substrate was weaker than internal adhesive strength [CITE:ev_1826db32940b1bde]. Adhesive failure occurs when adhesive peels away cleanly from substrate surface indicating poor surface preparation or contamination [CITE:ev_a1b14fef9fc9b89b]. Conversely, cohesive strength decreases as coating undergoes weathering and pull-off strength monitors breakdown, meaning cohesive failure within the coating layer indicates material degradation rather than bond failure [CITE:ev_8001a26420bf8b10]. Acceptable adhesion shows cohesive tear within sealant or elongation beyond movement capability, establishing that cohesive failure within the adhesive layer represents successful bonding [CITE:ev_52375915486842b0]. Glue failure involves visible separation of the glue from itself, the coating, or dolly, providing visual indicators for failure classification [CITE:ev_b53f0a2c5edbc39f]. Mechanical adhesion occurs when adhesive penetrates microscopic ridges and valleys of substrate anchoring the coating, distinguishing mechanical interlocking from chemical bonding mechanisms [CITE:ev_11cf92f65a363078].\n\nMaterial-specific performance varies significantly across epoxy, urethane, and silicone coatings. Nine common adhesives including cyanoacrylic and epoxy were selected and tested on specimens, providing comparative data across adhesive chemistries [CITE:ev_3f84be5fe88e4623]. Most cyanoacrylic adhesives failed with breakage occurring in the adhesive film during testing, indicating cohesive failure modes dominate for this material class [CITE:ev_90cd35013a72b096]. Two different epoxy adhesives were characterized including high elongation high toughness epoxy and toughened epoxy adhesive, demonstrating formulation variations affect failure modes [CITE:ev_61cc13e36041ee9a]. Adhesive formulations are designed to provide excellent adhesion and high shear and peel strength, with performance varying by polymer chemistry [CITE:ev_8711bd68ae7dca95]. Adhesion variance results from surface treatment or the nature of the substrate, emphasizing that test results reflect both coating and substrate conditions [CITE:ev_eb6a7ba09f91b0a1]. Coating application quality varies by coater and impacts adhesion, introducing human factors into test variability [CITE:ev_315057e2cc7dd3f8].\n\nField testing considerations differ from laboratory protocols. Field adhesion tests must be performed at the shop or jobsite to verify sealant adhesion, establishing location requirements for compliance [CITE:ev_5da5d464178b2eea]. Field adhesion test also called hand pull test verifies proper sealant installation, providing rapid on-site assessment capability [CITE:ev_7658ca702fb6e4fa]. Field adhesion test recommended when laboratory test cannot be performed on-site, acknowledging practical constraints in coating application environments [CITE:ev_f569492a9905b1d9]. Adhesion test must be conducted prior to estimating coating restoration project per Inland adhesion testing procedures, establishing timing requirements for assessment [CITE:ev_61c41c390dad6a70]. Paint and tape adhesion tests are useful for screening surface prep and cure on coatings, indicating these methods serve quality control functions rather than definitive strength measurement [CITE:ev_be43a72512267deb]. Independent laboratories have noted differences of up to 50 percent in tape adhesion variability over time, quantifying inter-laboratory variation that affects result comparability [CITE:ev_ce3124033536bdb1].\n\nAdhesion strength of coating to substrate defines overall coating integrity and performance, establishing the stakes for accurate testing [CITE:ev_2852c4416e3e0b87]. Evaluating adhesion before and after exposure lengths helps estimate adhesion retention, recommending longitudinal testing approaches [CITE:ev_02597b3988622977]. Mode of failure and pull-off pressure confirm proper surface preparation and coating application, linking test outcomes to process verification [CITE:ev_399c2dba04cb99b8]. Failure to verify and document adhesion results may result in adhesion loss not covered by warranty, establishing documentation requirements for liability protection [CITE:ev_a889a20cea5550f0]. All structural applications must be tested by the Dow test lab for surface preparation recommendations, indicating manufacturer-specific protocols for critical applications [CITE:ev_24d4477c6b68a302]. Adhesive bonds can be stronger than the materials being bonded together in some automotive applications, demonstrating that cohesive substrate failure represent optimal bonding performance [CITE:ev_20783c49fa127283]. Valid pull-off test requires coating to cover at least 50% of stamp surface after detachment, establishing validity criteria for quantitative data [CITE:ev_d0994f38fa60bebe]. Test method covers determination of practical adhesion strength of hard ceramic coatings on metal and ceramic substrates, extending applicability beyond polymer systems [CITE:ev_f6c7a8ecd3f37045]. Test method is commonly used for coating thicknesses in the range of 0.1 to 30 micrometers, defining dimensional constraints for accurate assessment [CITE:ev_8858c4a9dba538b2].\n\n**Key Findings**\n\n*   Surface contaminants such as oils, dust, or moisture form barriers between adhesive and surface, with solvent wiping increasing epoxy bond strength from 0.8 MPa to 10.9 MPa, demonstrating contamination control directly determines test outcomes [CITE:ev_4ef566dfab383143][CITE:ev_a3334666bd32dd8a].\n*   Pull-off testing provides quantitative values in MPa or psi for coating adhesion comparison, though evidence conflicts on whether it measures fundamental bond strength versus practical adhesion strength [CITE:ev_11ae17d7fc3f76c6][CITE:ev_d62d99d84a009e00][CITE:ev_1a095db8b38db71d].\n*   Cross-cut testing per ASTM D3359 (codified 1974) and ISO 2409 provides qualitative six-level classification, contrasting with pull-off quantitative assessment and limiting material comparison precision [CITE:ev_cee97c994fefc9b1][CITE:ev_067cc0abd42529d2][CITE:ev_cd6ffc422440dd18].\n*   Adhesive failure indicates weaker molecular attraction between adhesive and substrate than internal adhesive strength, whereas cohesive failure within the adhesive layer represents acceptable adhesion performance [CITE:ev_1826db32940b1bde][CITE:ev_52375915486842b0].\n*   Independent laboratories report up to 50 percent variability in tape adhesion testing, requiring multiple test methods and contamination controls for reliable epoxy, urethane, and silicone material comparison [CITE:ev_ce3124033536bdb1][CITE:ev_ac671c6a322fe8bd].\n\n![Measurement Specifications](data:image/png;base64,

 succeeded in 890ms:
   1: """I-carney-004 — static-accounts auth + HS256 JWT.
   2: 
   3: Pattern (Carney-demo scope):
   4: - Operator-curated YAML at `${POLARIS_STATIC_ACCOUNTS_PATH:-/app/config/static_accounts.yaml}`
   5:   with bcrypt-hashed passwords + role.
   6: - POST /auth/login → 12-hour HS256 JWT signed with POLARIS_JWT_SECRET.
   7: - `require_auth` FastAPI dependency injects on all routes except an allowlist
   8:   (/health, /transparency*, /auth/login).
   9: 
  10: LAW II: missing POLARIS_JWT_SECRET at app startup → fail-loud RuntimeError.
  11: Never silently allow unauthenticated traffic.
  12: 
  13: Phase-2: replace with Cognito/Okta when the demo gives way to long-term use.
  14: """
  15: 
  16: from __future__ import annotations
  17: 
  18: import os
  19: import secrets
  20: from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
  21: from pathlib import Path
  22: from typing import Any
  23: 
  24: import yaml
  25: from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, Request
  26: from fastapi.security import HTTPAuthorizationCredentials, HTTPBearer
  27: from jose import JWTError, jwt
  28: from passlib.context import CryptContext
  29: from pydantic import BaseModel
  30: 
  31: JWT_ALGORITHM = "HS256"
  32: JWT_EXPIRY_HOURS = 12
  33: 
  34: # Paths exempt from auth — health probes + reviewer-visible transparency.
  35: PUBLIC_PATH_PREFIXES: tuple[str, ...] = (
  36:     "/health",
  37:     "/transparency",
  38:     "/auth/login",
  39:     "/docs",       # FastAPI's own OpenAPI UI
  40:     "/redoc",
  41:     "/openapi.json",
  42: )
  43: 
  44: DEFAULT_ACCOUNTS_PATH = "/app/config/static_accounts.yaml"
  45: 
  46: _pwd_ctx = CryptContext(schemes=["bcrypt"], deprecated="auto")
  47: _bearer_scheme = HTTPBearer(auto_error=False)
  48: router = APIRouter(tags=["auth"])
  49: 
  50: 
  51: class LoginRequest(BaseModel):
  52:     username: str
  53:     password: str
  54: 
  55: 
  56: class LoginResponse(BaseModel):
  57:     access_token: str
  58:     token_type: str = "bearer"
  59:     role: str
  60:     expires_in: int  # seconds
  61: 
  62: 
  63: class User(BaseModel):
  64:     username: str
  65:     role: str
  66: 
  67: 
  68: def _load_accounts() -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
  69:     """Read the static-accounts YAML. Raises RuntimeError if missing.
  70: 
  71:     LAW II: never silently default to an empty account list (which would
  72:     behave as 'allow nobody' but the operator would not realize the file
  73:     was missing).
  74:     """
  75:     path = Path(os.environ.get("POLARIS_STATIC_ACCOUNTS_PATH", DEFAULT_ACCOUNTS_PATH))
  76:     if not path.exists():
  77:         raise RuntimeError(
  78:             f"static_accounts.yaml not found at {path}. Set "
  79:             "POLARIS_STATIC_ACCOUNTS_PATH or mount the file. The application "
  80:             "refuses to start without the auth substrate (LAW II)."
  81:         )
  82:     body = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
  83:     if not isinstance(body, dict) or not isinstance(body.get("accounts"), list):
  84:         raise RuntimeError(f"static_accounts.yaml at {path} must have top-level 'accounts' list")
  85:     return body["accounts"]
  86: 
  87: 
  88: def _jwt_secret() -> str:
  89:     secret = os.environ.get("POLARIS_JWT_SECRET", "").strip()
  90:     if not secret or len(secret) < 32:
  91:         raise RuntimeError(
  92:             "POLARIS_JWT_SECRET env var missing or too short (<32 chars). "
  93:             "Application refuses to issue tokens with a weak/missing key (LAW II). "
  94:             "Set via AWS Secrets Manager → cloud-init → .env per I-carney-004."
  95:         )
  96:     return secret
  97: 
  98: 
  99: def verify_app_startup() -> None:
 100:     """Called at FastAPI app create_app() time. Fail loud if auth substrate
 101:     is misconfigured. Per LAW II, we never start an auth-gated app with a
 102:     broken auth gate.
 103: 
 104:     Skipped when POLARIS_AUTH_DISABLED=1 (Phase-0 demo + tests).
 105:     """
 106:     if os.environ.get("POLARIS_AUTH_DISABLED", "") == "1":
 107:         return
 108:     _jwt_secret()
 109:     _load_accounts()  # raises if YAML missing or malformed
 110: 
 111: 
 112: def issue_token(username: str, role: str) -> tuple[str, int]:
 113:     """Returns (jwt, expires_in_seconds)."""
 114:     expires_in = JWT_EXPIRY_HOURS * 3600
 115:     payload = {
 116:         "sub": username,
 117:         "role": role,
 118:         "iat": datetime.now(timezone.utc),
 119:         "exp": datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(hours=JWT_EXPIRY_HOURS),
 120:     }
 121:     token = jwt.encode(payload, _jwt_secret(), algorithm=JWT_ALGORITHM)
 122:     return token, expires_in
 123: 
 124: 
 125: def _path_is_public(path: str) -> bool:
 126:     return any(path.startswith(prefix) for prefix in PUBLIC_PATH_PREFIXES)
 127: 
 128: 
 129: async def require_auth(
 130:     request: Request,
 131:     creds: HTTPAuthorizationCredentials | None = Depends(_bearer_scheme),
 132: ) -> User | None:
 133:     """Global FastAPI dependency. None on public paths; raises 401 otherwise.
 134: 
 135:     Phase-0 demo: POLARIS_AUTH_DISABLED=1 short-circuits to None (auth off).
 136:     """
 137:     if os.environ.get("POLARIS_AUTH_DISABLED", "") == "1":
 138:         return None
 139:     if _path_is_public(request.url.path):
 140:         return None
 141:     if creds is None or creds.scheme.lower() != "bearer":
 142:         raise HTTPException(
 143:             status_code=401,
 144:             detail={"error": "missing_bearer_token", "message": "Authorization: Bearer <jwt> required"},
 145:         )
 146:     try:
 147:         payload = jwt.decode(creds.credentials, _jwt_secret(), algorithms=[JWT_ALGORITHM])
 148:     except JWTError as exc:
 149:         raise HTTPException(
 150:             status_code=401,
 151:             detail={"error": "invalid_token", "message": str(exc)},
 152:         ) from exc
 153:     return User(username=payload["sub"], role=payload.get("role", "reviewer"))
 154: 
 155: 
 156: @router.post("/auth/login", response_model=LoginResponse)
 157: def login(payload: LoginRequest) -> LoginResponse:
 158:     accounts = _load_accounts()
 159:     for account in accounts:
 160:         if account.get("username") == payload.username:
 161:             hashed = account.get("password_bcrypt", "")
 162:             if hashed and _pwd_ctx.verify(payload.password, hashed):
 163:                 token, exp_in = issue_token(payload.username, account.get("role", "reviewer"))
 164:                 return LoginResponse(
 165:                     access_token=token,
 166:                     role=account.get("role", "reviewer"),
 167:                     expires_in=exp_in,
 168:                 )
 169:             break
 170:     # Constant-time-ish failure shape (don't leak whether username exists).
 171:     raise HTTPException(
 172:         status_code=401,
 173:         detail={"error": "invalid_credentials"},
 174:     )
 175: 
 176: 
 177: def generate_strong_secret(n_bytes: int = 48) -> str:
 178:     """Helper for operator scripts: 64-char URL-safe random secret."""
 179:     return secrets.token_urlsafe(n_bytes)

  45: _GOLDEN_RUN_INDEX = {
  46:     "golden_clinical_001": "golden_run_clinical.json",
  47:     "golden_housing_002": "golden_run_with_contradiction.json",
  48:     "golden_abort_003": "golden_run_abort_no_verified.json",
  49:     "golden_defense_004": "golden_run_defense.json",
  50:     "golden_climate_005": "golden_run_climate.json",
  51:     "golden_ai_006": "golden_run_ai_sovereignty.json",
  52:     "golden_with_drop_reason": "golden_run_with_drop_reason.json",
  53: }
  54: 
  55: 
  56: @router.get("/{run_id}/bundle", response_model=EvidenceContract)
  57: def get_bundle(run_id: str) -> EvidenceContract:
  58:     fixture_name = _GOLDEN_RUN_INDEX.get(run_id)
  59:     if fixture_name is None:
  60:         raise HTTPException(
  61:             status_code=404,
  62:             detail=f"Bundle for run {run_id!r} not found. Available golden runs: {list(_GOLDEN_RUN_INDEX)}",
  63:         )
  64:     raw = json.loads((_FIXTURE_DIR / fixture_name).read_text())
  65:     return EvidenceContract.model_validate(raw)
  66: 
  67: 
  68: @router.get("/{run_id}/bundle.tar.gz")
  69: def get_run_bundle_targz(
  70:     run_id: str,
  71:     sign_fn=Depends(get_sign_fn),
  72: ):
  73:     """Resolve run_id → artifact_dir → signed audit bundle (tar.gz).
  74: 
  75:     I-arch-001d Codex diff iter-1 P1-001: `Depends(get_sign_fn)` MUST use
  76:     the actual `get_sign_fn` callable from audit_bundle_route — the
  77:     create_app() registers `app.dependency_overrides[get_sign_fn]` to
  78:     inject the real GPGSigner when POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID is set. A lambda
  79:     Depends would be a different callable; the override would never fire.
  80: 
  81:     Status codes:
  82:         200: bundleable run; returns application/gzip
  83:         404: run not found, not completed, or artifact_dir missing
  84:         422: run aborted (pipeline_status=abort_*) or sovereignty cascade
  85:              emptied the report
  86:         503: GPG signer not configured (POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID unset) — surfaced
  87:              by the inner post_audit_bundle when sign_fn is None.
  88:     """
  89:     info = run_store.get_run(run_id)
  90:     if info is None:
  91:         raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail={"error": "run not found"})
  92:     if info.lifecycle_status != "completed":
  93:         raise HTTPException(
  94:             status_code=404,
  95:             detail={"error": f"run not completed: lifecycle_status={info.lifecycle_status}"},
  96:         )
  97:     if info.pipeline_status and info.pipeline_status.startswith("abort_"):
  98:         raise HTTPException(
  99:             status_code=422,
 100:             detail={"error": f"run aborted: pipeline_status={info.pipeline_status}", "bundleable": False},
 101:         )
 102:     if not info.artifact_dir:
 103:         raise HTTPException(
 104:             status_code=404, detail={"error": "run has no artifact_dir recorded"}
 105:         )
 106: 
 107:     artifact_dir = Path(info.artifact_dir)
 108:     if not artifact_dir.is_dir():
 109:         raise HTTPException(
 110:             status_code=404,
 111:             detail={"error": f"artifact_dir does not exist on disk: {artifact_dir}"},
 112:         )
 113: 
 114:     # I-arch-001d Codex diff iter-2 P1-002 fix: release_allowed gate.
 115:     # The slice-chain pipeline_verdict collapses partial_* into "success",
 116:     # but a release-blocked partial (e.g. partial_qwen_advisory with
 117:     # release_allowed=false) MUST NOT ship as a clean bundle. Read the raw
 118:     # manifest.release_allowed flag and refuse with 422 when False.
 119:     try:
 120:         manifest_raw = json.loads((artifact_dir / "manifest.json").read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
 121:     except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError) as exc:
 122:         raise HTTPException(
 123:             status_code=404,
 124:             detail={"error": f"manifest.json missing or invalid: {exc}"},
 125:         ) from exc
 126:     if not manifest_raw.get("release_allowed", False):
 127:         raise HTTPException(
 128:             status_code=422,
 129:             detail={
 130:                 "error": (
 131:                     f"run release-blocked: pipeline_status={info.pipeline_status!r}, "
 132:                     f"release_allowed=False. Bundle cannot ship until release gate clears."
 133:                 ),
 134:                 "bundleable": False,
 135:                 "pipeline_status": info.pipeline_status,
 136:             },
 137:         )
 138: 
 139:     try:
 140:         decision, pool, report = build_slice_chain(artifact_dir)
 141:     except SovereigntyFilterEmptiedReportError as exc:
 142:         raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail={"error": str(exc)}) from exc
 143:     except FileNotFoundError as exc:
 144:         raise HTTPException(
 145:             status_code=404,
 146:             detail={"error": f"artifact_dir incomplete: {exc}"},
 147:         ) from exc
 148: 
 149:     return post_audit_bundle(
 150:         AuditBundleRequest(decision=decision, pool=pool, report=report),
 151:         sign_fn=sign_fn,
 152:     )

  - "P1-3 is not fully resolved: docs/carney_demo_runbook.md §5 uses the Secrets Manager helper `sm()` for `/polaris/v6/openrouter_api_key`, `/polaris/v6/serper_api_key`, and `/polaris/v6/polaris_gpg_key_id`, but those are SSM Parameter Store entries in infra/aws/ssm_parameters.tf. `POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID` can be empty, making signed bundle export fail."
  - "P1-3 also still has an executable setup bug: `sudo bash -c \"sm polaris/v6/static_accounts_yaml > /etc/polaris/static_accounts.yaml\"` will not see the caller's shell function under sudo, so static_accounts.yaml may not be written and auth startup can fail."

  - "Q1: sign-off evidence ingestion approach is approved in principle; broaden the placeholder abort check to catch `<domain>`, `<sha>`, and `...` examples."
  - "Q2: mandatory egress lockdown §1b is approved; make the terraform-output command cwd-safe as a polish fix."

verdict: REQUEST_CHANGES
novel_p0: []
continuing_p0: []
p1:
  - "P1-3 is not fully resolved: docs/carney_demo_runbook.md §5 uses the Secrets Manager helper `sm()` for `/polaris/v6/openrouter_api_key`, `/polaris/v6/serper_api_key`, and `/polaris/v6/polaris_gpg_key_id`, but those are SSM Parameter Store entries in infra/aws/ssm_parameters.tf. `POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID` can be empty, making signed bundle export fail."
  - "P1-3 also still has an executable setup bug: `sudo bash -c \"sm polaris/v6/static_accounts_yaml > /etc/polaris/static_accounts.yaml\"` will not see the caller's shell function under sudo, so static_accounts.yaml may not be written and auth startup can fail."
p2:
  - "Q1: sign-off evidence ingestion approach is approved in principle; broaden the placeholder abort check to catch `<domain>`, `<sha>`, and `...` examples."
  - "Q2: mandatory egress lockdown §1b is approved; make the terraform-output command cwd-safe as a polish fix."
p3_cosmetic: []
convergence_call: continue
remaining_blockers:
  - "Fix fallback laptop setup to fetch SSM-backed values with `aws ssm get-parameter --with-decryption` and write static_accounts via a pipe to sudo tee, not a sudo subshell calling `sm`."
